title: Release DLL soft-locks when product autoload is only env/config armed
description: User hit a release/default ME3 soft lock after building with plain cargo xwin build --release. Static source showed title/native-menu visual suppression is default-on for real runs, but PRODUCT_AUTOLOAD_ARMED only armed when SaveLoadMethod::DirectMenuLoad plus the experimental direct-menu gate were selected. Agent smokes had been adding env/config gates behind the scenes, masking the plain-release failure.
close_reason: Release default arming, game-dir TOML, package audit, and ME3 profile pitfall documented/fixed

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Implemented static fix for the release/default soft lock.

Root cause from source inspection:
- `title_native_menu_visual_suppression_enabled()` was default-on for real non-telemetry runs.
- `PRODUCT_AUTOLOAD_ARMED` was only set when `SaveLoadMethod::DirectMenuLoad` plus the experimental direct-menu gate was selected.
- Agent smokes had been using env/config gates, so a plain release DLL could hide the native menu/cover while never starting `product_core_autoload_tick`.

Fix:
- `arm_product_autoload_from_request()` now arms `PRODUCT_AUTOLOAD_ARMED` for every real release/default attach unless explicitly disabled (`no-autoload`), telemetry-only, or native-profile-capture.
- Added repo instruction: product behavior must pass from a normal release DLL under ME3 with no hidden `RUNTIME_*`, `ER_EFFECTS_*`, or smoke-driver env vars.

Validation:
- `bash scripts/check.sh` PASS
- `cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` PASS
- Release DLL rebuilt at `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/er_effects_rs.dll`, size 1967104, mtime_ns 1783307170124518225.

Runtime proof status:
- Tried ME3 from the agent with no DLL env vars and only `--steam-dir /home/banon/.local/share/Steam`; ME3 resolved the profile and wrote an attach config for the release DLL, but did not reach an `eldenring.exe` process within 90s in this agent environment. No DLL telemetry was produced. Artifact log: `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/release-default-me3-no-env-long-20260705-200900.log`; ME3 log: `/home/banon/.local/share/me3/logs/quicksave/2026-07-05_20-09-00.log`.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Follow-up root cause from user manual ME3 soft lock:
- Live module list proved `er_effects_rs.dll` was loaded from `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/x86_64-pc-windows-msvc/release/er_effects_rs.dll`.
- DLL output was in the game directory, not DLL-adjacent: `/home/banon/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/ELDEN RING/Game/er-effects-telemetry.json` and `er-effects-autoload-debug.log`.
- Telemetry showed `product_autoload_armed=true`, so the prior arming fix was active.
- But `product_core_callsite_slot_ok_ticks=0`, `profile_slot_active_bytes_qword=0x0`, `oracle_corrupted_save_seen_count=1`, `oracle_corrupted_save_seen_id=401107`, `game_save_slot=-1`.
- ME3 log showed attach_config `savefile: Some("")`, caused by `/home/banon/Elden/quicksave.me3` containing `savefile = ""`.
- Removed that empty `savefile = ""` line from `/home/banon/Elden/quicksave.me3` so ME3 stops overriding default save handling with an empty alternative savefile.

title: Experiment temporary 240 FPS TitleNativeJobTiming during autoload
description: User idea: test whether raising the title/native job frame-rate constant from the current 60Hz-derived TitleNativeJobTiming frame rate to ~240Hz only during the pre-world/autoload path can speed character loading. Scope: make it gated/temporary, restore normal timing after the character/world load is complete, and validate with the full runtime oracle so this does not hide failures or overfit. Investigate whether this changes frame_delta-driven menu/title/load pumps safely versus causing physics/timing side effects.
close_reason: Informal 240-FPS nudge showed no meaningful speedup; keep FrameRate at 60

title: Same-slot foreign-save switch reloads the resident character (return-title save clobbers the commit)
description:
User-reported 2026-07-06 (run seamless-save-smoke-20260706-144801, two different .co2 picks): choosing a foreign save's character in the SAME slot index as the resident character reloads the resident character -- the actual world character, not just the loading screen. FACE-IDENTITY MISMATCH #1 fired at the build kick (the new semaphore caught it in RAM).

ROOT CAUSE (log-proven): system_quit_save_swap_prepare_selected_slot commits the foreign bytes at slot activation (+98.921s), then the native return-title chain sets saveRequested and the game SAVES (+99.31 bc4=1 -> +99.34 save_state=1) -- rewriting the ACTIVE slot + profile summary into the active file over the commit. A same-slot pick therefore deserializes the original character; different-slot picks always survived because only the active slot's USER_DATA entry is rewritten (this is why every earlier switch QA passed).

FIX: re-commit the candidate bytes at the bc4-terminal point in title_tick_cover.rs (save provably finished, fresh deserialize still seconds away) via system_quit_save_swap_recommit_after_return_title_save(), idempotent per switch (recommitted latch, reset at each activation commit). No data loss: System-Quit already saved the old character into their own file before ProfileSelect; the return-title re-save was landing in the wrong (foreign) file anyway. Related: er-effects-rs-7q7 (timing-dependent save-dir).
close_reason: Fixed + runtime-validated twice (runs seamless-runtime-smoke-20260706-150435 and seamless-co2fix2-smoke-20260706-151021): same-slot foreign pick now loads the picked character (Da BEAST slot0 over resident slot0). Fix = re-commit the candidate bytes at bc4-terminal in title_tick_cover.rs after the game's return-title save finishes (system_quit_save_swap_recommit_after_return_title_save, idempotent via recommitted latch). Log shows commit -> game save -> RE-commit ordering both runs.

title: Loading portrait shows previous character on System-Quit switch load (stale bake bridge)
description:
User-reported 2026-07-06 (run jsm-slotstats2-switchqa-20260706-125259): second character load's loading screen kept showing character 1's animated head; stats text (fixed same day) updated correctly.

EVIDENCE (RAM/pixel, no screenshot reading): window-2 published 192 clean keyed frames from the correctly-kicked slot-1 renderer (kick #2, model live, anim bound), yet the window-2 2056x2056 RT dump structure-correlates 0.92 (48x48 luma grid) and 0.98 palette-intersects with window-1's character-1 dump -- same model, different anim phase. Telemetry: oracle_profile_renderer_spare_hits=1 (teardown-spare hook fired only at window 1), oracle_profile_spare_orphans_deleted=0, oracle_ownership_spared_outstanding=1 at run end.

ROOT CAUSE: loading_portrait_window_reset parks the spared prior-character renderer in PROFILE_SPARE_ORPHAN, whose only reclaim site was profile_renderer_teardown_spare_hook (native CSMenuProfModelRend teardown-all) -- which the System-Quit switch path never fires. The orphan's model+offscreen scene stayed registered, rendering char 1's head every frame through window 2; the readback published it. Related but distinct from er-effects-rs-qh7 (ac0 lag, addressed by portrait_target_slot).

FIX: reclaim the orphan (delay_delete_enqueue_renderer + ownership_release) at the switch confirm in system_quit_arm_quickload_autoload, with a backstop in maybe_build_profile_table_for_loading before the new window's builder runs; swap(0) keeps sites mutually exclusive; the teardown-spare hook reclaim remains for the Continue path.
close_reason: Fixed + user visual sign-off (run portrait-faceid2-switchqa-20260706-143508): second loading screen shows the picked foreign character. Root cause was the foreign-preview record writer cloning the ORIGINAL save's record as a template and only overwriting name/level/playtime -- FaceData/ChrAsm stayed the original character's. Fix: walker-based extraction of the foreign character's FaceDataBuffer + runtime-layout ChrAsm image from the save's variable-length sections (fixed runtime offsets false-negatived on every slot), written via native FaceData::CopyFromBuffer + ChrAsm copy. Face-identity semaphore live: oracle_portrait_face_identity_checks=1 mismatches=0 on the passing run. Also shipped in the same arc: clear-on-complete of the published head buffer (no stale bake/bridge), post-draw overlay-landing probe (96-97% head-match), orphan reclaim at switch confirm + table build.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
ROOT-CAUSE CORRECTION (run 2, portrait-orphanfix-switchqa-20260706-131119): the orphaned-spared-renderer theory is REFUTED. Run 2 had spare_hits=0 (no spared renderer ever existed, so no orphan; the confirm/table-build reclaim sites correctly found orphan=0 and the fix code was verified present in the artifact DLL) yet the user saw the identical wrong head.

PIXEL PROOF the readback is INNOCENT: cross-run same-character baseline corr(run1.slot0, run2.slot0)=0.9995 (48x48 luma grid) vs corr(slot0, slot1)=0.92 within each run -- window 2's readback content is OUTSIDE the same-character band, i.e. it IS the new character. The publish pipeline produced char-2 frames (352 fresh overlay re-uploads, 0 GPU failures) while the user saw char 1.

ACTUAL MECHANISM (display side): the now-loading background is forge-baked ONCE per artwork decode from LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA, which at window-2 decode still held char 1's make-before-break bridge frame (kept by design, user 2026-07-03). maybe_update_gfx_loading_portrait is a no-op (re-forge removed), so the baked char-1 art persists all window. The Present-overlay's char-2 draws have NO on-screen proof (head_probe_count=0; the existing probe is gated on GFX_PORTRAIT_BAKED>0 which stays 0, and samples pre-draw anyway) -- whether the overlay lands is UNVERIFIED.

FIX APPLIED (user-directed 2026-07-06, reversing 2026-07-03 make-before-break): loading_portrait_window_reset now CLEARS LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA + PROFILE_HAVE_KEYED_FRAME at load completion, so no stale character exists to bake or bridge; next window starts head-less until the new character's first keyed frame. Plus: unconditional POST-draw backbuffer head-match probe (mutually exclusive with the candidate-A pre-draw probe) so the next run yields a RAM semaphore for whether the overlay head actually reaches the presented frame. The orphan reclaim at confirm/table-build is KEPT (real leak when the spare engages: run 1 leaked one undeleted renderer; historical GX-queue overflow at ~switch #4).

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
TRUE ROOT CAUSE FOUND (user visual ID 2026-07-06: rendered dumps showed BANON in all three windows, incl. the slot-1 window). The foreign-save ProfileSelect preview (system_quit_apply_foreign_profile_summary_preview -> write_profile_summary_record) clones a FALLBACK RECORD from the ORIGINAL save's first active slot as a structural template, then overwrites ONLY name/level/playtime/runes/map. The record's FaceData (+0x38) and ChrAsm (+0x1a8) -- what the portrait renderer builds the model from -- stayed the ORIGINAL character's (Banon). So the pipeline faithfully kicked/built/rendered/published the right SLOT with the WRONG character's face+equipment; stats text was correct because name/level ARE overwritten. This also retroactively explains the earlier 0.92-0.98 cross-window pixel correlations (Banon-from-record vs live Banon).

FIX (build-validated, awaiting runtime QA): write_profile_summary_record now fills the real visual blocks from the FOREIGN character's PGD via the game's native helpers -- FaceData::CopyFromBuffer (0x252f70; saved wrapper header differs from live, so never memcpy) + ChrAsm copy (0x245c00) + gender/archetype/gift/c4 bytes -- mirroring the proven seed_profile_summary_slot_from_staged_save; RVAs promoted to shared constants (player_correctness.rs).

FACE-IDENTITY SEMAPHORE (user directive: the run must detect a wrong rendered character itself): the preview stores an FNV-1a64 fingerprint of each written slot's inner FaceDataBuffer (PROFILE_PREVIEW_FACE_HASH, reset at preview start + snapshot restore); kick_target_profile_slot re-hashes the record at build-kick time; drift bumps oracle_portrait_face_identity_mismatches (+ _checks) and logs 'FACE-IDENTITY MISMATCH'. Telemetry-only per default posture; watchers can fail-fast on it.

Kept from earlier this session (all pending the same QA run): clear-on-complete of LOADING_BG_PORTRAIT_RGBA (no stale bake/bridge reservoir), post-draw overlay-landing probe, orphan reclaim at confirm/table-build.

title: Loading-screen stats show first character during second character load
description:
User-reported 2026-07-06: the loading-screen player-stats text (er-effects-rs-jsm feature) does not update when loading a second character via System Quit -> Load Profile; character 1's stats render through character 2's whole loading screen.

Root cause (two compounding defects in src/experiments/startup_hooks/stats_loading_text.rs):
1. read_loading_screen_stats() used ac0-first portrait_loaded_slot_confirmed(); during a switch ac0 still names the resident OLD character until deserialize, and the still-resident char-1 PGD name/level-validates against char-1's record -> a 'live' char-1 bitmap builds at window start.
2. maybe_build_stats_text() one-shot STATS_TEXT_LIVE latch re-armed AFTER stats_text_window_reset() (force_profile_render_tick keeps ticking until load_done+cover-down), re-latching char-1 live stats; load 2 then skipped every rebuild.

Fix: slot source switched to the make-before-break priority (SYSTEM_QUIT_QUICKLOAD_SELECTED_SLOT first, then ac0/stepper, then best_active_slot), and the bitmap is now CONTENT-KEYED (rebuild iff formatted lines differ from the cached ones) so stale builds self-heal; no per-window latch. STATS_TEXT_BUILT is now a cumulative build-count oracle.
close_reason: Fixed: content-keyed stats bitmap + selected-slot-first read (commit pending push). User visual QA passed 2026-07-06 on no-teardown switch run jsm-slotstats2-switchqa-20260706-125259: stats updated correctly for the second character.

title: EPIC: gate DLL feature behavior on Seamless co-op module detection
description: STATUS 2026-07-06: Seamless autoload works end-to-end (feature/seamless-compat, live-validated -- real char to world, zero-input, no crash/ToS/MessageBox/picker). Wired via seamless_coop_loaded() (GetModuleHandleA co-op module): (DONE) co2 save target + .sl2/.co2 fallback; (DONE) ToS-skip + co-op post-PAB MessageBox-skip (advance the FixOrderJobSequence job to Success); (DONE) sticky-latch detection; (DONE) crash fix. Deployment note: main checkout is on another branch so the me3 seamless profile points at the seamless-integration worktree DLL. REMAINING/optional (not blocking the validated result): online-disable per-frame in-world clear scoping, input-block watchdog, title-cover-under-coop, msgbox observe-only -- all still filed; the ToS/MessageBox appeared nondeterministically so those skips are a safety net.

title: Seamless: input-block never releases when autoload stalls -> user stuck with no control (add watchdog)
description: Audit finding (POSSIBLE) + LIVE-OBSERVED. The DInput8 GetDeviceState + XInputGetState/Ex block (input_block.rs, input_blocker.rs) is armed by default because autoload is armed; it zeroes real input until in-world (release keyed to player presence). Under co-op the autoload stalls on the wrong-save bug and in-world is never reached, so the block never releases -- observed 2026-07-05: player_available=false after 200s, game covered + uncontrollable. FIXES: (1) when seamless_coop_loaded(), do not arm the boot autoload until it is proven against the .co2 flow -- un-arming autoload un-arms the block for free; (2) UNCONDITIONALLY add a bounded watchdog that calls release_input_block_now() if in-world is not reached within N seconds, so a stalled autoload can never leave the user unable to play. simulated_button_presses_total stayed 0 (zero-input contract intact).

title: Seamless: autoload targets ER0000.sl2 not .co2 -> loads default char, stalls, leaves game covered+input-blocked
description: STATUS 2026-07-06 (feature/seamless-compat, commits 7b7cb49 co2-target, ad8853e ToS-suppress+latch, dd5af19 gap-fix): (1) active save extension resolves co2 under co-op via sticky-latched seamless_coop_loaded(); (2) DllMain default-save existence gate now falls back across .sl2/.co2 (default_save_file_names) so a co2-only profile enters DEFAULT-USER-SAVE instead of the boot missing-save picker -- co2-only gap CLOSED; (3) world-load rides native Continue (game reads own save, module-redirected to co2), so our reads are display-only, no contamination. Vanilla path unchanged. All built + fmt-clean + pushed. LIVE co-op validation still PENDING -- blocked on runtime (concurrent vanilla-probe session removes the co-op module); awaiting user go-ahead. Success semaphore: 0 TosTitle builds + boot-view->CONTINUE + co2 read + world, zero MessageBox/input, no crash.
close_reason: DONE + LIVE-VALIDATED 2026-07-06 (feature/seamless-compat b9465d5). Under real Seamless the zero-input autoload loads a real .co2 character to the world: no crash, no ToS, no MessageBox (oracle_msgbox_total_builds=0), no missing-save picker, simulated_button_presses=0. DLL-gated on co-op-module detection (seamless_coop_loaded), no env/config. co2 target + .sl2/.co2 default-save fallback + foreign-SteamID acceptance (validated_save_file_path is existence+size; load normalizes id) all confirmed. Full chain fixed: swapchain crash (fd1bde4) -> co2 target (7b7cb49) -> ToS ctor-suppress+sticky-latch (ad8853e) -> co2 fallback (dd5af19) -> job-sequence advance past ToS + co-op post-PAB MessageBox (b9465d5).

title: Seamless compat: BFS swapchain fallback latches foreign/stale swapchain under ERSC and self-present crashes
description: Crash repro 2026-07-05 21:37 (~/Elden/launch.sh -s, me3 quicksave-seamless profile, ersc.dll updated 21:19, er_effects_rs.dll built 21:05 from loading-screen-refinements@8021296). VEH crash log: AV at +831ms, RIP=0x6ffffc9005eb (dxgi.dll code, 0x405 before resolved Present8=0x6ffffc9009f0), rcx=0 fault_addr=0 read. PDB-symbolized call chain: present_overlay::boot_present_pump -> gpu_readback::composite_boot_progress_self_frame -> composite_boot_progress_inner -> first swapchain method call faults. Debug log delta vs same-build vanilla runs: vanilla latches swapchain 0xc191e0 via g_GxDrawContext chain (tries 34-36, ~+950-1100ms, no BFS line); Seamless run logged 'FOUND game swapchain 0x8d77e0 (vtable=0x6ffffcc388f0) via BFS after 12897 objs depth=12' at +831ms then crashed on first self-present. Root cause: find_game_swapchain_bfs accepts ANY heap object whose vtable[8]/[22] match the shared DXVK CDXGISwapChain vtable; under Seamless, ERSC's own DXGI activity leaves a swapchain-shaped (likely released dummy) object reachable from garbage pointers, its internal state dead -> first GetCurrentBackBufferIndex/GetBuffer derefs null. Fix: never latch a BFS-found object as GAME_SWAPCHAIN; trust only the RE-confirmed g_GxDrawContext chain (BFS removed, throttled miss diagnostics added). Degraded mode on future chain drift = no overlay, never a crash.
close_reason: FIXED + LIVE-VALIDATED (2026-07-05 22:11, feature/seamless-compat commit fd1bde4). Removed the BFS swapchain fallback in find_game_swapchain; the RE-confirmed g_GxDrawContext output-vector chain is now the only accepted source (miss -> None -> retry next frame; degraded mode = no overlay, never a crash). Live proof under Seamless via me3: DLL attached, present-overlay VMT-swap game swapchain 0xc17a40 tries=63 resolved via the chain (NO via-BFS line), boot-pump self-presented, game PRESENT(8) hook fired + handed over cleanly, boot progressed GAME->OFFLINE->TITLE->MENU, process ran 200s+ with ZERO access-violation. Prior crash was AV at +831ms on a dead BFS-latched swapchain object.

title: Fit loading portrait to full screen bounds
description: User report: custom-rendered loading-screen character portrait does not fit to screen. Ensure the clipping mask / visible bounds cover the entire viewable screen, and experiment with 4x supersampling up to that resolution to improve render FPS/quality.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Follow-up per user refinement: no runtime launch. Changed loading portrait overlay to aspect-cover scale/crop (no stretch), removed 4x supersampling, and changed native profile offscreen target to 56x56 with supersample flag forced off. Static validation only: cargo fmt --check and cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc passed.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Added objective playback-rate semaphores for loading portrait choppiness; no runtime launch. New telemetry: oracle_overlay_draw_first_ms/last_ms/draw_fps_x1000, oracle_overlay_reupload_first_ms/last_ms/reupload_fps_x1000, oracle_overlay_stale_present_current/max. Interpretation: draw_fps proves composite-to-swapchain rate; reupload_fps proves distinct portrait source-frame playback rate; stale_present_max proves held-frame runs even if the overlay keeps drawing. Static validation: cargo fmt --check and cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc passed.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Runtime playback semaphore result for 56x56 aspect-cover probe: /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/loading-portrait-56-fps-20260705-140710. Evidence: overlay_draw_hits=52, overlay_reuploads=51, draw_fps_x1000=1230 (~1.23 FPS), reupload_fps_x1000=1230 (~1.23 FPS), stale_present_max=1. This proves the visible portrait is updating far below 60 FPS; stale frames are not accumulating inside a fast overlay loop, instead the whole overlay/source update path is only reaching the swapchain about 1.23 FPS. 56x56 native source did not improve the measured portrait playback rate.

title: RE: default remembered sort option per menu
description: Investigate how Elden Ring persists menu sort selections across character loads but resets on process/power cycle, then identify DLL-safe hooks/state writes to default armaments, talismans, and armor menus to order-of-acquisition rather than item type when each menu opens. Static RE first; runtime tracebreakpoint probes only after static candidates are grounded. User has live game available for attach-only probes.
close_reason: Implemented configurable menu sort defaults and user-verified armaments/armor/talismans Order of Acquisition ascending behavior

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Static RE checkpoint for default menu sort investigation:

- Branch: feature/menu-default-sort-order.
- SortMenu builder: dump 0x1407fa410 -> deobf 0x1407fa320, loads GFX 02_032_SortMenu.
- Sort caller flow: row+0x8 is the current/session-persisted sort option. If negative, vanilla resolves MenuEquipTableData[row[0]].+0x10 as the default sort option. The same effective option feeds both the displayed selected option (r8d to SortMenu) and adapter object via 0x14084f1c0, so changing the fallback/default before adapter build keeps UI and comparator consistent.
- Order of Acquisition is FMG GR_MenuText id 6190 / 0x182e and sort/comparator id 0x5140 / 20800. Screenshot labels line up with table type 4: Item Type 6105, Order of Acquisition 6190, Weight 6111, Attack Power 6130, Guarded Damage Negation 6140.
- Static rows: Armaments MenuEquipTableData row 0x29 @ 0x143b37428 has label 40550 and sort type/default +0x10 = 4; Armor row 0x2a @ 0x143b37460 has label 40551 and type/default +0x10 = 6. Talismans likely resolve through category 40509 and sort type 8, but exact row needs one more bounded trace/static mapping.
- Candidate implementation direction: when opening target sort menus, if effective row+0x8 < 0 or row default is item-type, write 0x5140 to row+0x8 before 0x14084f1c0, or patch relevant static row +0x10 fallback(s). Prefer RAM write/hook over data patch until talisman row and unintended categories are proven.

Artifacts:
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/subagents/menu-sort-static-context.md
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/subagents/menu-sort-assets-and-ids.md

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Implemented menu sort default boot write and smoke-tested. Static RE: CSMenuSystemSaveLoad::field_0x1440 is a 24-entry remembered sort array; FUN_1408581c0 returns field_0x1440[sort_type] & 0x7fffffff, high bit is direction. Constructor initializes entries to 0x80005141 (Item Type) except known special defaults; order-of-acquisition is 0x5140. Added one-shot write for sort types [4 armaments, 6 armor, 8 talismans] only when the current low bits are still Item Type, preserving any non-default/user state. Runtime proof: /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/menu-sort-defaults-full-20260704-203234; me3-smoke-verdict reports watcher_pass=true, dll_attach=true, game_task=true, crash_free=true. Debug log line: [+15475ms] menu-sort-defaults: applied order-of-acquisition defaults mss=0xac690080 changed=3 already=0 skipped=0 targets=[4, 6, 8]. First live run proved armaments/armor but talismans failed; static follow-up found talismans use SortMenu type 9 (option list 0x143b35f50..0x143b35f80), not type 8. Second live run proved all three categories selected Order of Acquisition but direction was inverse; final fix writes 0x80005140 for Order of Acquisition. User confirmed final run: weapons, armor, and talismans sort as desired. Final artifact: /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/menu-sort-manual-direction-20260704-213642. title: Repeat slot switch trips portrait identity fail-fast
description:
Repro/context from no-teardown manual run:
- User loaded a save from another arbitrary save file, then after the character loaded in, tried to load another slot from that same save file.
- Crash occurred on fade-out / second load transition.

Artifact:
/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/no-teardown-loose-same-dll-save-20260704-192759

Evidence:
- Process was absent after the user-reported crash.
- No er-effects-crash.log was written, but the last DLL debug line is a portrait identity fail-fast:
  PORTRAIT-IDENTITY-SEMAPHORE FAIL: target_slot=0 record(name_len=5 map=0xe000000) vs loaded(name_len=16 map=0x23000000) name_match=false map_mismatch=true
- Immediately before that, the second continue/load path fired:
  system-quit-quickload: fresh picked-slot deserialize OK #2 slot=0 -- forwarding continue_confirm so SetState5 streams
- Source in src/experiments/startup_hooks/loading_cover_save_slot.rs documents that this semaphore deliberately faults when crash logging is enabled.

Initial classification:
- Not evidence against initial arbitrary save-file direct_file redirect: first smoke/load passed and direct staging was valid.
- Likely repeatable profile-switch/portrait identity path bug: after switching to another slot from the same configured save, the target slot record and live loaded character identity diverge, tripping the fail-fast during fade/stream.
- Needs a focused fix/probe around slot selection/record targeting for repeated switches, not save redirect fallback.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Implemented arbitrary configured save-file support on refactor/file-size-sanity.

Behavior change:
- `save_file` may now be any readable `.sl2`/`.co2` path >= 1MiB. It no longer has to live under an `EldenRing/<steamid>` directory.
- Actual save-file opens (`.sl2`/`.co2`) redirect to the configured file itself.
- Directory/existence probes still redirect to a private staging tree next to the configured file (`er-effects-save-redirect-stage`) and lazily populate `eldenring/<active steamid>/er0000.sl2` when the game's native path reveals the SteamID. This keeps native save discovery happy without requiring the user's path to contain SteamID or EldenRing.
- Backup `.bak` paths redirect beside the configured file.

Validation:
- python3 scripts/check-rust-file-sizes.py: PASS
- cargo fmt --check: PASS
- cargo xwin check --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc -p er-effects-rs: PASS
- git diff --check: PASS

Note: Rust does not have a native UTF-16 bytestring literal; current path filters still use u16 ASCII constants/helpers. A later cleanup can collapse those into an ascii-wide helper/macro, but the user-facing blocker is fixed in the redirect logic.

title: me3 launch path for production smoketest: load er_effects_rs.dll as me3 native, verify settings stick
description: Goal: figure out if/how to launch Elden Ring via me3 (0.11.0 installed) with er_effects_rs.dll and verify our flag-file/env settings still apply. Not needed for regular testing; needed for user's production smoketest. Branch: feat/me3-launch-smoketest. Findings so far: me3 binary has no steam-URL/applaunch forms and no EAC-launcher reference; it drives the Steam compat tool directly with the Proton waitforexitandrun verb against Game/eldenring.exe, so it is the approved direct-launch class. Remaining: (1) DLL flag-file resolution (CWD vs exe dir) under me3; (2) native load timing vs LazyLoader chainload; (3) telemetry oracle proof that settings applied; (4) gated smoketest script.

title: ErStats push crash: first execution UAFs in SetText wrapper at in-world ProfileSelect open
description:
Run system-quit-repro-pause-at-menu 2026-07-04 07:08 (HEAD 8942ea1 + pause-at-menu harness): first-ever execution of the assign-driven ErStats push (push_stats_text_on_row, startup_hooks.rs ~7730) crashed ER with 0xc0000005 at +37836ms, 5ms after the 10 face-bind redirects, during the FIRST in-world System->Load-Profile ProfileSelect open. No stats-text log line ever printed (crash before push's own log; title-phase populate never runs the trigger, so in-world open is the path's first execution). Crash chain (deobf RVAs, matches our constants exactly): SetText wrapper 0x74a000 (PROFILE_SETTEXT_RVA) entry does rcx=*field; call *0x8(vt) -- ret 0x74a012 on stack; the stale *field pointer 0x7ffe9e52cc90 (menu-job arena; == our submitted 05_010 wrapper job 0x7ffe9e52cc80 + 0x10, freed after pump consumed it) has heap garbage vt 0x7ffe9858e8e0; the bogus vt+0x8 slot dispatched into ~CSScaleformValue 0xd7f850 (ret0 0xd7f88c = its call *0x10(rax) at 0xd7f889), which jumped to .rdata 0x142cbf2dd -> AV. fault_addr 0x96f05bc8 = low-32 truncation of heap ptr. Row-populate FUN_1408758d0 (dump) in stack (deobf ret 0x875814). So: assign resolve for 'ErStats' returned out!=0 but out+0x8 (SCENE_OBJ_PROXY_SCALEFORM_VALUE_OFFSET) held a dangling pointer into the freed job allocation. Open questions: (1) does assign leave the value slot unwritten/garbage when the child is MISSING from the movie instance (was the in-world 05_010 instance really our edited movie? runtime_edit_serves=1, served at +14853ms title phase; in-world AcquireMenuResource total=15 may have reused a cached instance) (2) is the proxy layout for a resolved DefineEditText child different (value not at +0x8)? Fix directions: validate the resolved value (vtable in .rdata range + dataType) before calling SetText; decompile binder not-found path; log assign's out vs our buffer. Evidence: target/runtime-probe/system-quit-repro-pause-at-menu/{er-effects-crash-log.txt,er-effects-autoload-debug.log}. NOT az9 (different signature/region; az9=repeated-switch 0x1ae GX region).

## Context
Found by the new pause-at-menu harness (ER_EFFECTS_SQ_REPRO_SWITCHES=0) on its first proof run -- user predicted the crash-before-pause outcome.
notes: ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED via A/B (2026-07-04). The crash is NOT the ErStats push (that never runs: oracle_stats_text_row_populates=0). It is the NATIVE ProfileSelect row-populate FUN_1408758d0 (deobf 0x8758d0), which UNCONDITIONALLY resolves 'Icon_0' and 'Icon_0/m_trialFaceIcon' via assignComponentWithName, operates on them (FUN_140749a80/FUN_14074bdb0), and releases the resulting CSScaleformValue via ~CSScaleformValue (0xd7f850). Our stats-panel 05_010 GFX edit REMOVED Icon_0's PlaceObject from the row template (title_05_010_edits.rs edit#3: 'Icon_0'->'ErStats' placeobject; make_05_010_stats.rs row.remove(icon)). With Icon_0 unplaced, the resolve yields an invalid value and ~CSScaleformValue's 'call *0x10(rax)' hits a garbage vtable -> AV at .rdata 0x142cbf2dd. The generator comment 'Icon_0 setters are dataType-guarded so unplaced is a safe no-op' is FALSE. PROOF: staged er-effects-no-stats-panel.txt (stats-panel OFF, Icon_0 vanilla-present) -> harness navigated to ProfileSelect, sq_repro_paused_at_profile_select=1, state=DONE, ER ALIVE 40s+ paused, ZERO access-violation. FIX (user direction 2026-07-04: OMIT the boxes, don't show them): keep Icon_0 a resolvable PLACED instance but alpha-0 (CxformWithAlpha mult [256,256,256,0]) so it renders nothing and frees the strip for text -- native resolve/release stays valid. Regenerate movie+edit table+EDITED fingerprint.

(2) 05_010 GFX edit via er_gfx::title_05_010 (6 content-addressed edits: Icon_0 face box removed, char67 deco repurposed as 630x40 22px MenuFont_01 ErStats EditText at (-300,15), PlayerName/Level-caption/Level moved left into freed strip), served in-place at Scaleform file-open (mirror of 05_000 runtime strip). title: ProfileSelect stats panel: blank render + register per-slot texture + redirect binds
ProfileSelect stats panel: CPU stats-panel texture (bitmap font + DdsImage)
Switch feed-deserialize save-dir is timing-dependent; portrait per-frame work masked it (softlock when portraits off)
description:
ROOT (2026-07-03, exposed by disabling the portrait pipeline, run br93fa9dv): the System->Quit switch's continue_confirm hook drives a fresh feed-deserialize of the picked slot before SetState5 (startup_hooks.rs ~10128, own_load_feed_deserialize, fail-closed). Its save-dir resolution is TIMING-DEPENDENT: the game's save-dir path-DB field populates LATE (after submit -- see own_stepper.rs:497 'pre-submit population REFUTED' + the cold-char-mount SAVE-DIR notes). When continue_confirm fires BEFORE the path-DB is populated, own-load falls back to the default AppData dir (C:/users/steamuser/AppData/Roaming/EldenRing/{steamid}/) which is EMPTY in a staged/redirected-save run -> 'own-load: no .sl2/.co2 file under dir ... cannot read save' -> deserialize FAILS -> continue_confirm BLOCKS (correctly refuses to stream stale) -> product-core-autoload waits forever at owner_state=10 -> SOFTLOCK on switch #1.
TRIGGER: disabling the portrait pipeline (four render gates default-OFF, commit b67f351, REVERTED bb63c08). The portrait per-frame work (force_profile_render_tick each frame) was incidentally providing TIMING SLACK that delayed continue_confirm until the save-dir was populated, so the feed-deserialize read succeeded. Removing it fires continue_confirm earlier -> empty AppData -> fail. So the switch autoload is NOT independent of portraits (my 'cosmetic-only' claim was WRONG). lib.rs:198 explicitly says the DLL must NEVER read the default user save dir -- the AppData fallback is the bug.
FIX (proper, unblocks disabling portraits): make continue_confirm's feed-deserialize either (a) READINESS-GATE on the save-dir path-DB being populated (poll [io20+0x20]/the dir field != default before reading, retry until ready), or (b) read the REDIRECTED/staged save path directly (ER_EFFECTS_SAVE_FILE / the save_redirect resolution) instead of resolving via the timing-dependent game path-DB. Then portraits can be disabled without the softlock. Do NOT rely on portrait per-frame work as a timing crutch. Verify with a portraits-off 10-switch smoke.

title: Loading portrait: intermittent per-window publish starvation freezes prior character through the load
description: Soak fence-relax-1 (2026-07-03, 10 switches, commit c6df0fc): with the #1 drive freeze fixed (all 11 windows animated ~1:1 with display), a few windows still FROZE visually on the PRIOR character through the whole loading screen (user-observed windows 5, 6, 11 = transitions 3rd->4th, 4th->5th, 9th->10th; all other windows flawless live animation). Mechanism: publish-side starvation -- the new model's frames never pass the publish gate, so the make-before-break bridge holds the previous character's last clean frame. Window 6 is attributed: tear-gate storm, EVERY keyed frame scored tear=39-40 > threshold 10 (65 rejects, first-ever torn skips of the run; suspiciously CONSTANT score suggests a persistent seam, e.g. two producers writing the RT or a stable mid-write row, not stochastic scanline noise). Windows 5 and 11 are UNATTRIBUTED: no torn/pin/incoherent lines; suspects are an unkeyed streak (depth pairing failing all window -> frames never keyed; skipped_unkeyed=608 cumulative) or multi-model stable-gate skips (multi=366 cumulative) -- the cumulative oracles cannot localize. INSTRUMENTATION ADDED (this commit): loading_portrait_window_reset now logs per-window publish[clean/torn/unkeyed/multi/pin_moves/fence_skips] deltas; a frozen window shows clean=0 + its dominant skip class. NEXT: rerun the 10-switch soak, read the frozen windows' class, then fix that class at the root (tear storm -> real readback sync vs game render; unkeyed -> depth-pairing repair after retarget invalidate).
notes: VALIDATION SOAK (2026-07-03, commit a6f3358 target-slot binding): PASSED on every window it reached before az9 killed the run at switch #4. All 4 completed windows published clean with ZERO torn / ZERO unkeyed: boot 175, win2 309, win3 298 (this exact window class stalled at clean=92 wrong-identity in the attribution run), win4 243 (this class had the torn=214 storm). Both prior failure classes (frozen-on-prior identity publishes + constant-39/40 tear storms) are GONE with the target-slot binding. Run ended by er-effects-rs-az9 (same 0x1ae-region Scaleform-teardown AV, +170480ms, right after retarget slot3 -- switch #4, matching both prior firings). Residual work on THIS issue: none identified; keep open until a full 10-switch soak completes crash-free (blocked on az9).
close_reason: CLOSED on provenance soak v2 (commit 76897d8, 2026-07-03 ~21:04): full 10-switch soak, crash-free, ALL 11 windows published the correct character live (clean 105-298/window), zero unpaired/scan-provenance publishes (src[color bundle=*/scan=0 depth chain=*/bfs=0] every window -- the bundle chain never missed), zero full-window freezes, zero green-face. The frozen-on-prior-character bug class is resolved by the stack: target-slot binding (a6f3358) + strict bundle-provenance publish gate (c8c0225/76897d8) + drive/teardown fence (c6df0fc) + MZ-vcall scan hardening (235f613). User verdict: 'pretty good, with one very tiny exception' = a single ~0.5s missing-depth-mask moment, spun off as its own issue (unkeyed-streak residual, windows 10/11 unkeyed=31 each; user hypothesis: Patches boot/menu-path renderer lifecycle differs on reload).

title: Repeated-switch ProfileSelect Scaleform teardown use-after-free crash
notes: ATTRIBUTION CORRECTED (2026-07-03 static+PDB forensics): the LAST TWO 'az9' firings (+154662ms on a3d63b4, +170480ms on a6f3358 -- garbage RIP 0x300905a4d, stack 0x1aeaa9f/0x1af1411) were NOT this Scaleform-teardown bug. PDB symbolization of the self frames: ret0 = gpu_readback::find_d3d12_resource_ex+0x642, caller readback_offscreen_fast_coherent+0x214 -- OUR portrait readback's D3D12 heap scan. Root: the scan's in_ranges(vt,&d3d) vtable check accepted vt == d3d module BASE, so a freed heap chunk reused to hold a d3d module HANDLE passed as a 'd3d object' and the QueryInterface vcall executed the PE header as code -- crash RIP 0x300905a4d is literally the 'MZ\x90' signature bytes (0x5a4d='MZ'). The game-side 0x1ae stack RVAs are an allocator/pool path under the scan (disasm: call 0x141aeae60 alloc + store), not Scaleform teardown. FIXED: d3d_vtable_ok in find_d3d12_resource_ex now requires vtable AND its QI slot >= 0x1000 into a d3d module before any vcall. Ghidra ground truth also corrected the hook target naming: FUN_1411aaf10 is a scalar-deleting destructor (calls dtor FUN_1411a8920 then allocator-frees), not a per-frame Run body. WHAT REMAINS OF az9 PROPER: only the ORIGINAL firing (fault_addr=0x0 inside FUN_1411a8920's intrusive-list walk, run stable-base1-noteardown-20260703-120137) -- unreproduced since the GX-overflow/spared-renderer fixes (48afa18) across 24+ switches. If the next soaks stay clean with the scan hardening, close this as (a) original list-walk crash plausibly a downstream casualty of the since-fixed renderer leak family, (b) recent firings = the now-fixed scan bug.
close_reason: CLOSED after root-cause + crash-free proof (2026-07-03). (1) The recent firings (+154662/+170480, garbage RIP 0x300905a4d) were NOT this bug: PDB symbolization = our portrait readback D3D12 scan vcalling through a d3d module BASE stored in a reused freed chunk (RIP is the 'MZ\x90' header bytes). Fixed by d3d_vtable_ok hardening (commit 235f613): vtable + QI slot must sit >=0x1000 into a d3d module. Proof: full 10-switch soak on 235f613 completed CRASH-FREE (11 windows). (2) az9 proper (the single FUN_1411a8920 intrusive-list dtor fault, run stable-base1) has not reproduced across 30+ switches since the GX-overflow/spared-renderer leak fix (48afa18) -- consistent with it being a downstream casualty of the since-fixed renderer-leak family. Ghidra correction recorded: FUN_1411aaf10 is the scalar-deleting dtor (dtor + allocator free), not a per-frame Run body. Reopen ONLY on a new fault INSIDE FUN_1411a8920 with fault_addr=0x0 list-walk signature.

title: Loading portrait: cross-slot RT swap + early pop-out (IN_WORLD false positive)
description: Run strip-default-drive-20260702-194018: (1) portrait swaps to another save-slot character mid-load: engine rebuilds ALL 10 slot models post-Continue (summary marks persist from menu; global refresh builds every marked slot despite mark-only-target), and readback_offscreen_fast picks largest-BFS-reachable RT fresh each frame -> flips to foreign slot RT when late slots (7/8/9) finish building. (2) portrait pops out at +27.8s via IN_WORLD_REACHED (PlayerIns loading-screen false positive), before loading bar fills; also latches forever killing portraits on subsequent reloads. Fix: suppress non-target model builds in our post-Continue window (renderer +0x754/755 latches), pin readback candidate once confirmed non-checker, replace overlay stop with NowLoadingHelper-update-cessation window logic.

title: Silence PAB + Continue activation SFX during product autoload title flow
description:
User requirement 2026-07-02 (follow-on to er-effects-rs-dl0): the title flow still PLAYS SOUNDS on press-any-button advance and Continue confirm even though the visuals are now stripped. Silence exactly those activation sounds on the product zero-input path. NO prior sound RE exists in bd (user-confirmed 2026-07-02).

TRIGGER SITES (what fires them): (1) PAB advance -- readiness pab-advance hook drives the native PAB node-update 0x1407ad1c0 / accept path; (2) Continue confirm -- native title accept byte -> TitleTopDialog in-update open-menu, and continue_confirm wrapper 0x140b0e180 (also the System-Quit clean-title path). Elden Ring audio is Wwise-based (sd/ .bnk banks); menu UI SEs are likely fired natively via a play-sound-event helper with an SE id from the decide/OK handlers (menu decide/cancel SE ids possibly data-driven via a menu param), OR movie-driven from the (still-running) 05_000 AS3 shell -- determine which first.

INVESTIGATION (static first, Ghidra dump ermaporch has real names): (a) symbol sweep for Sound/PlaySe/SoundEvent/CSSound in the persistent project; (b) decompile the PAB dismiss + open_menu/continue_confirm paths and find the sound call + its id argument; (c) map deobf addresses via scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py before any hook/patch. PRODUCT LEVER SHAPE: suppress only those SE plays while the product autoload drives the title (tie to the same autoload-state gate as the strip; no global mute, no audio-device tampering, save-safe, zero-input preserved). Add a RAM telemetry counter oracle_title_se_suppressed (or similar) so runs prove the suppression fired. ACCEPTANCE: product boot with autoload -> no PAB/Continue activation SFX audible (user confirms), all other audio unaffected, T_controllable + zero-msgbox + zero-input oracles unchanged.
notes: First-pass RE done 2026-07-02 (see bd memory title-activation-sfx-native-not-movie-2026-07-02): sounds are NATIVE Wwise, NOT movie (stripped movie has 0 sound tags + 0 AS3 sound refs). PlaySE/CSSoundImp cluster is world/positional layer, wrong for menu. continue_confirm has no sound call -> menu decide handler plays it on accept-byte transition. Remaining: runtime trace at AK PostEvent (event-id + caller_rva) during title to pin the two ids, then whitelist-suppress. Ghidra GUI locked the headless project; used MCP.

title: Product-default in-memory stripped 05_000_title.gfx: kill title-UI animate-in drag at the asset layer
description:
# Goal

Eliminate the title-UI animate-in cost (PRESS ANY BUTTON, CONTINUE menu, footer/copyright, version block) at the ASSET layer: serve a stripped `05_000_title.gfx` from memory at Scaleform load time so the visual placements never exist and never animate, instead of hiding each element after it is built. User steer 2026-07-02: "Many of these assets are usually on disk and we edit them in memory" -- verified below; treat direction as confirmed, but validate each sub-claim during implementation.

# Measured drag to eliminate (run 2026-07-02 18:55, debug log timestamps)

- Second title visit (System-Quit switch): native `SetState(title, state=2)` +42303ms -> "PRESS BUTTON component ready; armed native title accept byte" +45297ms (**2.99s** of title build/animate before the product can arm accept) -> continue_confirm forwarded +46964ms (**+1.67s** more). ~**4.7s** title overhead per character switch.
- Cold boot: 05_000_title.gfx file-open +15135ms -> bind/build +15.6s -> menu portraits baked +17168ms -> T_controllable +27153ms.
- Part of the 3s is GFX/FMG disk load + build, part is the FadeIn/menu-open/footer animate-in; the existing title-anim levers already skip FadeIn->Loop (log +15641ms, hook 0x140b0d400/0x1407499e0) and speed pab_dismiss->menu_open 4x (`TITLE_ANIM_SPEEDUP_DEFAULT`, `src/constants.rs:452-472`), so the REMAINING drag is dominated by what still animates: menu items, footer, version block. Asset-strip removes those timelines entirely. Measure before/after via the same log events (SetState(2) -> accept-armed -> continue_confirm deltas) as the RAM-telemetry proof.

# Machinery that already exists (all verified in-repo)

- Embedded stripped asset: `src/title_05_000_text_suppressed_bytes.rs` -- `TITLE_05_000_TEXT_SUPPRESSED_GFX`, "Generated from target/custom-gfx-lab/title-text-suppressed/05_000_title.native-ui-stripped.gfx. Stripped 05_000_title.gfx: removes visual title/menu/text/progress placements while preserving the native GFx shell for game interactivity." Included via `src/constants.rs:1965`.
- In-memory replacement path: Scaleform MemoryFile-backed file-open replacement (`SCALEFORM_MEMORY_*` consts `src/constants.rs:1947-1963`, selection in `src/experiments/startup_hooks.rs:66,114`), currently opt-in via env `ER_EFFECTS_TITLE_RESOURCE_MEMORY_GFX` (`src/experiments/gating.rs:881-885`). Run evidence the hook rides the right path: file-open observer sampled `data0:/menu/05_000_title.gfx` from disk at +15135ms.
- Diagnostic embedded variants (`embedded:minimal-magenta`, `embedded:minimal-magenta-counter`, `src/constants.rs:1967-1988`) prove the MemoryFile path renders and can carry a frame-counter marker for recorder-independent timing.

# Gating / policy constraints

- **No new env-gated features** (bd user pref): promote from `ER_EFFECTS_TITLE_RESOURCE_MEMORY_GFX` opt-in to product default TIED TO the existing autoload/product enablement state, mirroring how `title_native_menu_visual_suppression_enabled()` de-gated. Keep env only as explicit override.
- Branch interaction (`src/lib.rs:276-351`): today memory-GFX mode and Part-A suppression are mutually exclusive branches (`title_native_menu_visual_suppression_enabled()` returns false when memory-GFX is on, because old TitleBack hide hooks would hide the replacement surface). Product default must re-decide which per-element hooks remain as defense-in-depth (bind-hide of stray names, logo hooks) vs which conflict.
- Probe contract: `ER_EFFECTS_TITLE_RESOURCE_MEMORY_GFX` is listed in VISUAL_RESOURCE_MUTATION_ENVS (`scripts/check-runtime-probe-contract.py:337`); flipping default-on moves that mutation into every product run -- update the contract/tests deliberately (they must change together with the policy per AGENTS).
- Human-drive diagnostic runs (System-Quit repeatable switching) currently rely on the VISIBLE vanilla menu for the human to drive. Product-default strip must keep a diagnostic escape (existing env override suffices) and must not break menu INTERACTIVITY (stripped shell preserves the native GFx shell for game interactivity -- validate accept/menu-open still function with zero input).
- Do not regress: zero simulated input, zero MessageBoxDialog builds, save-safety, Seamless-Co-op compatibility (bd `autoload-dll-product-requirements`); title cover surfaces must be provably above PRESS ANY BUTTON / CONTINUE (AGENTS; related open issue er-effects-rs-trp).
- Verify the stripped asset actually covers the footer CopyrightText placement and decide 05_020_TitleInformation (version block) handling -- see the footer bug ticket.

# Acceptance sketch

1. 2. Loading-screen-portrait screenshot captured at the portrait-cover moment for USER review (agents do not read it).
3. `scripts/check.sh` + probe-contract tests updated and green.

Reload-title visual re-check rides the next System-Quit run (movie is cached from the same replaced MemoryFile). Rust-native regeneration tracked in er-effects-rs-h7x.

title: [bug] Title footer CopyrightText + 05_020 version block never hidden (bind names never traverse hooked binder)
description:
# Symptom (user-reviewed frames, run 2026-07-02 18:55)

The title footer -- the copyright line "(c)Bandai Namco Entertainment Inc. / (c)2024 FromSoftware, Inc." -- is visible in BOTH user-reviewed frames (`frame-000854.jpg`, `frame-000911.jpg` under `/home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/system-quit-reload-patches-noteardown/human-drive-20260702-185536/frames/`), and it animates in (user-observed), taking time to load and contributing to title-phase drag. Frame 911 additionally shows the bottom-right version block ("Shadow of the Erdtree / OFFLINE / App Ver. 1.16.2 / Calibrations Ver. 1.16.1").

# Why the existing hides miss it (verified this run)

1. **CopyrightText never traverses the hooked binder.** The named-child bind hook (0x14074a2f0, `title_scene_obj_proxy_named_child_bind_hook`) match-list includes `CopyrightText`, `StaticSystemText_101000`, `StaticSystemText_100100`, `PRESS BUTTON`, `Info`, `ProgressInfo`, `Install_ProgressInfo`, `PressStart` (`src/experiments/startup_hooks.rs:6149-6159`), but the debug log shows only 4 names ever bound through it (PressStart, Info, ProgressInfo, Install_ProgressInfo -- calls 1-4 cold boot at +15606ms, calls 5-8 second title at +42369ms). `CopyrightText` and both `StaticSystemText_*` names NEVER hit the hook in the entire run => the footer text object is created/bound through a different native path.
2. **05_020_TitleInformation cover hook installed but never fired.** Log +121ms: "hooked PAB/TitleInformation wrapper 0x14081f8d0; native 05_020_TitleInformation preserved and covered", yet telemetry `oracle_title_pab_information_visual_builds=0` / `any_built=false` while the version block IS on screen (frame 911). The version/info block reaches the screen via a path the wrapper hook does not see.
3. The catch-all for both would have been the 05_000_Title render-suppression, which never engaged this run (window latch 0x0 -- see the PAB/menu bug ticket).

# Investigation leads

- Static RE first (per AGENTS): find who binds/creates `CopyrightText` -- search the binary for the "CopyrightText" ASCII/UTF-16 string and xrefs (Ghidra persistent project `ermaporch`, `scripts/ghidra-query.sh`; ground any patch/call address with `scripts/dump-deobf-shift.py`). Compare with the binder 0x14074a2f0 callers to learn which sibling function binds footer children.
- Determine which movie hosts the footer: 05_000_title.gfx placements (the embedded stripped GFX generator claims to remove "title/menu/text/progress placements" -- verify CopyrightText placement is among the stripped ones in `target/custom-gfx-lab/title-text-suppressed/05_000_title.native-ui-stripped.gfx` provenance) vs 05_020_TitleInformation.gfx (version block).
- Confirm whether the version block is data-driven into 05_020 or a separate movie; the 0x14081f8d0 wrapper hook's build path clearly is not the one used on this flow (builds=0) -- find the real one.

# Fix directions

- Preferred: asset-level removal via the in-memory stripped-GFX replacement (feature ticket) so footer + version block never exist; verify the stripped 05_000 actually strips CopyrightText, and produce/strip 05_020_TitleInformation equivalently if the version block lives there.
- Fallback: extend the bind/build hooks to the real footer bind path found by RE.
- Add an `oracle_*` RAM semaphore proving footer/version placements are absent (e.g. count of live named text objects in the title movie), so probes can score this without screenshots.
close_reason: Superseded/resolved by er-effects-rs-dl0 asset-level strip: footer CopyrightText and the version block placements live in 05_000_title.gfx and are absent from the served stripped movie -- user-reviewed v1/v2 runs 2026-07-02 showed neither (v1's only residual was the Cursor glow, removed in v2). The unanswered RE question (which binder path CopyrightText used; why 05_020 wrapper hook saw builds=0) is recorded in the issue body for historical reference; no product need remains.

title: System-Quit switch: 3rd ProfileSelect open crashes (DAT_143d6d8d0 null preview-model)
description:
When our cloned in-world Load-Profile switch opens the native ProfileSelect a THIRD time in one session, the game crashes. Crash log 2026-07-02 (explore run, session aa5f5d1a): access-violation game+0x9aa6d4 reading [null+0x754], via MenuWindowJob::Run (0x7ad2bc) -> FUN_140820d50 -> FUN_14081ebc0 -> FUN_1409aa7d0. Process exit 0xC0000005.

ROOT CAUSE (Ghidra ermaporch dump): FUN_1409aa7d0 (dump; = deobf 0x1409aa6d4 region) is the per-frame ProfileSelect character-preview builder. It walks the 10-entry pointer array DAT_143d6d8d0 (one CSMenuProfModelRend per save slot, size 0xa30) and dereferences *(entry+0x754) WITHOUT null-checking the entry. On the 3rd open one entry is null -> [null+0x754] AV. Lifecycle: FUN_1409af4f0 (ProfileSelect SETUP) calls FUN_1409b2f00 (TEARDOWN: frees all 10 via CSDelayDeleteMan->FUN_140e77540, nulls them) then re-allocs 10 via HeapAlloc(0xa30,0x10,GLOBAL_GfxHeapAllocator); on alloc failure it stores 0 (uVar1=0) into DAT_143d6d8d0[slot]. So the null is either (a) a GfxHeap alloc failure on the 3rd setup, or (b) the per-frame builder running in the teardown->realloc null window. Our feature is the only thing that reopens ProfileSelect in-session (vanilla shows it once at boot), so we expose this latent native bug.

STATUS: found while user manually drove a 3rd switch during explore mode (SQ_REPRO_TARGET_SWITCHES=1 build). Blocks the 'drive our feature to load a third character' goal. NEXT: diagnostic to determine null origin (alloc-fail vs teardown/update race) via a driven 3-switch repro, then fix (ensure DAT_143d6d8d0 fully populated when the builder runs -- prefer root-causing the null over a bare null guard). Related: [[system-quit-gaitem-singleton-restore-RESOLVED-2026-07-02]] (same class: per-slot native resource degrading across our repeated in-session reopens).

title: Investigate ProfileSelect slot activation from in-world System menu
description: Follow-up from er-effects-rs-srz. Presentation/interactivity of System -> Quit Game -> Select profile to load is validated, but actual slot activation/load from that in-world ProfileSelect route was intentionally kept out of scope and earlier attempts crashed around live RVA 0x67141a. Investigate native semantics for selecting a slot from this injected ProfileSelect context, save-safe with staged gold save, and preserve zero accidental load behavior until explicitly gated.

First it reads ProfileSelect cursor via 0x140739e20(dialog+0xa38), gets selected summary id, and submits a GR_Dialogues 0x631fb Decision confirmation dialog. The actual confirmed transition is the downstream lambda_4c99 operator at dump 0x1409a4ee0 -> deobf/live 0x1409a4d90; it writes *(dialog+0x1cc8+0x14c)=2 and dialog+0x1e8=Success, which lets downstream load/deser run. Telemetry showed system_quit_profile_load_activate_count=1, oracle_msgbox_total_builds=1, but confirmed lambda block/allow counts stayed 0, so the 0x1409a4d90 lambda is not the pre-crash boundary. Static RE of the queued load job found MenuJobWithContext<LoadJobContext>::Run at dump 0x140826e40 -> deobf/live 0x140826d50 (vtable 0x142ac71e0+0x10). The job object stores the ProfileSelect list pointer at job+0x50 and profile id at job+0x58. I added a save-safe hook there: for the injected System Quit ProfileSelect (list == profile_window+0x50 and real windows hidden), accepting confirmation now returns MenuJobResult(Failed,0) and does not call the native load job unless ER_EFFECTS_SYSTEM_QUIT_ALLOW_PROFILE_LOAD=1. Static/runtime evidence so far: in-world ProfileSelect display + native confirmation works; directly letting LoadJobContext::Run deserialize in-world crashes at CSGaitemImp::Deserialize 0x14067141a. The normal Quit Game path queues return-title teardown (FUN_14094d9f0 -> FUN_14079d2e0 -> accepted callback FUN_14067a490 sets GameMan+0xbc4/teardown flags). Next RE should determine whether we can run that teardown invisibly/behind cover, wait on native safe-state semaphores, then submit profile load without showing title; do not assume visible title is required.

If forced to visibly return to the main menu and replay the whole startup/menu sequence, the feature is much less valuable. Static RE note: existing autoload path is not “do startup”; it uses TitleTopDialog/TFC and selector/dispatcher (dump 0x1409a9000 -> 0x1409b31c0) to build the native Continue/load job after setting mss+0x1200 slot and tfc+0x14c. comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Subagent synthesis for er-effects-rs-8vq: six read-only agents converged on the same product-preserving path. Existing Continue/autoload path is the proven load executor: live TitleTopDialog owner+0xe0, TFC at dialog+0xa38, set mss+0x1200 to selected slot, set tfc+0x14c=LOAD, clear tfc+0x18c, call selector/dispatcher (deobf 0x1409a8eb0 -> dump 0x1409a9000; deobf 0x1409b3070 -> dump 0x1409b31c0), install returned job into owner+0x130. Current System ProfileSelect path is safe only as slot-selection/confirmation: direct LoadJobContext::Run in-world reaches CSGaitemImp::Deserialize and can crash because gaitem lifecycle/entry table is not in title-clean state. Deserialize-specific semaphores to add before any direct allow: gaitem global *(0x143d69890) non-null, gaitem+0x19028 == 0, gaitem+0x19030 == 0, gaitem+0x19031 == 0, per-entry table/vtable/index valid before the 0x14067141a vcall. Recommended implementation: keep blocking the in-world ProfileSelect load job, record selected slot/profile_id, trigger/arm native quit-to-title/teardown semantics and let existing Continue autoload take over as soon as title owner/safe state exists, while proving no visible title replay via interval-scoped telemetry. Visible title return is fallback only, not target UX. Output artifacts: /tmp/er-effects-rs-8vq-subagents/continue-path.md, crashing-profile-load.md, return-title-teardown.md, deserialize-safety.md, integration-plan.md, probe-design.md.

title: Investigate System menu Load Character option
description: User goal: in-game Esc -> System -> Quit Game tab should show a third option beside Quit Game / Return to Desktop that opens the character-selection/load-game view, avoiding return to startup/title menu. Initial static finding: this is likely native menu/model work, not primarily GFX asset work. Quit Game tab category is built by FUN_14093c0e0 / OptionSettingTopDialog path; the two buttons are added in FUN_140958a00 via CS::MatchingDialog::AddCancelButton and callbacks that enqueue quit-to-title and quit-to-desktop job chains. ProfileLoadDialog/load-game view factory is already known at live/deobf 0x14081ead0 (dump 0x14081ebc0) and existing er-effects code has fire_live_loadgame_node/factory handling. Glorious Merchant source zip at target/elden-ring-glorious-merchant-main.zip is a useful reference for MsgRepository text hooks, pattern scanning, and menu/talkscript mutation, but it mostly patches talk menus/shop params rather than the System menu.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Static RE checkpoint for System->Quit Game Load Character feature:

- Glorious Merchant source confirms a useful text-overlay pattern: hook `MsgRepositoryImp::LookupEntry` and return owned/static wide strings for selected `(msgbnd,msg_id)`; it does not patch the System menu directly.
- For this feature, first-pass text probably does not need FMG parsing: existing FMG IDs already cover usable labels: `GR_MenuText 288000 = Character Selection`, `GR_LineHelp 288000 = Select which character to use`, `GR_MenuText 406000 = LOAD GAME`.
- The in-game Quit Game tab is not a pure GFX surface: `OptionSettingTopDialog` adds a category from `FUN_14093c0e0` (`GetGR_MenuTextEntry(110500)`, category id 9). The tab content is built by `FUN_140958a00`.
- `FUN_140958a00` calls `CS::MatchingDialog::AddCancelButton` twice. First label helper `FUN_140957110` = `GR_MenuText 110510 Quit Game` + `GR_LineHelp 110500`; its action chain eventually calls `FUN_14094e9f0` -> builds quit-to-title chain via `FUN_14079d7f0` and dialog `GR_Dialogues 110000`. Second helper `FUN_1409570c0` = `GR_MenuText 110511 Return to Desktop` + `GR_LineHelp 110501`; action chain calls `FUN_14094d9f0` -> `FUN_14079d2e0` and `GR_Dialogues 110010`.
- `AddCancelButton`/`FUN_140920bc0` creates an `EditProperty`, initializes a `PropertyNewButtonController`, and pushes into `PropertyEditDialog.properties` (`DLFixedVector<EditProperty>` cap 16, so a third button is structurally plausible).
- Feasibility risk: constructing the third button needs valid MSVC `std::function` objects. The native stack objects in `FUN_140958a00` are cloneable through their native vtables (`0x142b12b48` quit-title action, `0x142b12bb8` desktop action, `0x142ac7760` bool predicate). We can either (A) call `AddCancelButton` with native-like std::function objects and detour the cloned action object for our third row, or (B) implement a tiny compatible function/vtable shim in Rust. Do not assume this is a GFX edit.
- ProfileLoadDialog/load character target remains the existing known factory path: deobf/live `0x14081ead0` (dump `0x14081ebc0`) and existing `fire_live_loadgame_node`/`LIVE_DIALOG_FACTORY_RVA` code.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Static RE update: simplest third-button proof path found.

Instead of constructing our own MSVC `std::function` or touching FMG/GFX, hook `CS::MatchingDialog::AddCancelButton` live/deobf `0x140920c90` (dump `0x140920d80`) and duplicate ONLY the second call from the Quit Game tab builder.

Evidence:
- Quit tab builder dump `FUN_140958a00` -> live/deobf `0x140958910`.
- First AddCancelButton call returns to live RVA `0x958a20` after adding `Quit Game`.
- Second AddCancelButton call returns to live RVA `0x958b37` after adding `Return to Desktop`.
- At the hook point all caller stack objects are still alive; destructors run only after the call returns (`MenuHelpLabelComponent` and std::function destructors follow the callsite). Calling the original trampoline a second time from the hook with the exact same five args should append a third row with the same label/help/action/predicate as the second native button.
- `PropertyEditDialog.properties` is `DLFixedVector<EditProperty>` capacity 16, count at `dialog+0x1af0`; original has only two rows, so a third row is structurally safe.
- This proof avoids the hard part: no custom callbacks, no FMG replacement, no ProfileLoadDialog/save semantics. It only proves the UI can fit and dispatch a third native button.

Recommended proof gate: enable by explicit env/file flag, duplicate the second button only, verify the System->Quit Game tab shows three rows and that the third opens the same native Return-to-Desktop confirmation dialog (do not confirm the dialog during proof).

title: Native-shader object draw: ISG1 parser (er-shaderkit) + real FLVER passthrough draw + readback (er-objectkit)
description: Next milestone after er-flver. (1) er-shaderkit: parse the .vpo DX container ISG1 chunk -> Vec<Isg1Input{semantic_name, semantic_index, register}> (ISG1 confirmed present in ER DXIL containers). Feeds er-flver::RawVertexBuffer::match_isg1. (2) er-objectkit/passthrough.rs: upload raw FLVER vertex buffers verbatim, build pipeline via RawVertexBuffer::wgpu_layout(sig), synthesize engine cbuffers (MVP from studio camera) + material cbMtdParam, bind material textures, indexed draw of a real mesh, read back a NON-BLANK image proving geometry rendered through the native vpo+ppo. Open deps from er-flver spec open_risks: validate 0x2E=Snorm16x4, biased-byte (b-127)/127 vs hw snorm against a real .vpo decode, uvFactor=2048, parallel multi-buffer binding. Engine/material cbuffer field OFFSETS need the DX container RDEF chunk (cbuffer layout) — separate sub-task. Needs assets extracted (target/er-objectkit empty now; loader auto-extracts via wine bridge). Pick a base-game model+material+pass whose matbin resolves to a base-game .shaderbdle (c4800 was DLC-only).

title: Create er-flver crate: raw FLVER vertex declarations for native-shader passthrough render
description: Factor FLVER handling out of er-objectkit into a dedicated er-flver crate. Headline capability: expose RAW interleaved vertex buffers + full per-member D3D-style declarations (semantic, semantic_index, format code, struct_offset, stride, count) + a FLVER-format-code -> wgpu::VertexFormat mapping, so real FLVER geometry can be bound to the native compiled .vpo vertex shader (matched against its ISG1 input signature) for passthrough rendering. Keep a normalized decode (positions/normals/uvs/tangents) for the Bevy PBR / studio-camera path. Harvest from user's flveRS crate (github.com/chozandrias76/flveRS, src/helpers/flver_asset.rs) and fstools_formats FLVER reader. Host-only, pure Rust, no Oodle (decompression stays in er-soulsformats wine bridge). Unblocks the M3 native-shader render frontier.
notes: er-flver crate scaffolded + wired. Modules: error/semantic/format/layout/vertex/mesh + lib. WRAP fstools_formats reader (catch_unwind panic boundary in parse_structural). Two paths: parse()->ObjectModel (normalized, ported verbatim from old er-objectkit/flver.rs) and parse_raw()->RawFlver (raw interleaved buffer slices + VertexMember decls + map_format authoritative format table + match_isg1 ISG1 reconciliation; optional wgpu feature for VertexBufferLayout). er-objectkit keeps a 'pub mod flver { pub use er_flver::*; }' alias so loader/scene/material/viewer need ZERO edits. Compiling now.
close_reason: er-flver crate created, tested (13/13), and verified. Wraps fstools_formats reader (panic-safe catch_unwind boundary). Two paths: parse()->ObjectModel (normalized, ported verbatim) and parse_raw()->RawFlver (raw interleaved buffer slices + VertexMember declarations + authoritative semantic-keyed map_format format table + match_isg1 ISG1 reconciliation; optional wgpu feature builds wgpu::VertexBufferLayout). er-objectkit keeps 'pub mod flver { pub use er_flver::* }' alias -> zero edits to loader/scene/material/viewer; both build green. Full scripts/check.sh green (fmt + no-lossy-utf8 + cargo xwin DLL check unaffected; er-flver stays host-only).
