comments.text:
Gap inventory for the concrete portrait-cover plan:

A. Input-blocker gap (must be fixed before product proof): current no-hudhook baseline only completes with the new DInput ilhook install skipped. This is not acceptable final proof because it weakens user-input suppression. Need root-cause/fix src/input_blocker.rs DInput hook install/probe so keyboard+mouse suppression is active without crashing/hanging, then rerun the baseline.

B. Black-cover ownership gap: 01_900_Black can be built statically via wrapper 0x140793b20, but we still must prove its MenuWindowJob is actually above the native title/PAB/Continue layer for the whole init interval. Current native baseline can already produce a big black screen/footer with no custom black job, so black pixels alone are not proof. Need RAM oracle + event screenshot/pixel analyzer that distinguishes custom 01_900_Black surface from native title background and rejects visible footer/text/logo.

C. Surface-control gap: 01_900_Black may be a static GFx asset with no simple fill-color control. Need static RE of its Scaleform tree/value handles, or a custom in-memory/minimal GFX/resource-load hook. Success is not “black appears”; success is changing the same surface to an unmistakable agent-chosen color and proving it on screen.

D. Custom GFX/resource gap: if 01_900_Black cannot be recolored/repurposed, we need a no-loose-file, no-hudhook way to serve an embedded custom GFX/resource into the native MenuWindowJob loader. Need identify the resource/load hook boundary and prove lifetime/order matches BeginTitle/PAB.

E. Portrait source-vs-visibility gap: existing CSMenuProfModelRend/SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00 telemetry proves a portrait texture source can exist, not that pixels are drawn. Need a visible root-reachable image symbol on the custom surface and a valid bind owner/path that consumes SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00.

F. Bind-owner ABI gap: prior raw TitleBackViewParts bind attempts crashed/failed. Need prove the correct owner layout for bind helper 0x1407451c0: ProfileSelect-like MenuWindow owner field +0x3b8 with owner+0x60 initialized, or an equivalent custom surface owner. Do not call bind helper against raw TitleBackViewParts+0x8 until this is statically proven.

G. Root-reachable symbol gap: 05_010_ProfileSelect exports MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10 but they are not root-reachable; binding them does not make pixels visible. Any custom/reused GFX must place the target symbol in the display list, and tooling must verify root reachability before runtime.

H. Proof-oracle gap: the current logo-replacement screenshot trigger only fires for portrait-cover oracles, not for black/color surface proof. Need add a separate event semaphore/capture for black/color cover and later portrait binding, with exact target-window validation and pixel/native classification. Screenshots remain diagnostic; final stop/score must come from RAM/native/pixel oracle fields.

I. No-hudhook regression gap: hudhook/debug must stay removed. Any plan step that seems to need overlay/DX12 draw must be rephrased as native Scaleform/MenuWindow/resource work or rejected.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
CONSOLIDATED IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (from 8 headless read-only investigation lanes, 2026-06-28)

Baseline/constraints:
- Baseline commit: 0f668ab (pushed) = no-hudhook title baseline.
- Do NOT re-add hudhook or debug. Any render path must be native Scaleform/MenuWindow/resource work.
- Current runtime-happy baseline artifact: target/runtime-probe/product-continue-direct-20260628-105043. Important caveat: it completed only because DInput ilhook install is skipped; this is not final product proof.

R1 — Fix the no-hudhook input blocker first (mandatory product-proof prerequisite)
- Finding from headless lane A: likely root cause is the new DInput ilhook live patch race. Vendored MinHook freezes other threads before patching; ilhook does not. A function the input thread is executing can run half-written bytes -> AV/hang, and catch_unwind cannot contain SEH AVs.
- Implementation direction: remove ilhook from the DInput GetDeviceState hook path. Keep the existing probe-device address discovery, but install keyboard/mouse GetDeviceState detours through src/mh.rs / MinHook, matching the XInput path that already works.
- Required code shape: static extern detours for keyboard and mouse GetDeviceState; AtomicUsize trampoline(s); call original trampoline then zero buffer / injected key. Hook keyboard_addr and mouse_addr with MhHook::new + queue_enable + MH_ApplyQueued. Do not reintroduce hudhook::mh.
- Gate: cargo xwin build; runtime probe with DInput block active; telemetry/log must show DInput keyboard+mouse hook installed, XInput hook installed or retried, zero simulated input, no crash, no MessageBoxDialog.
- Gap closed when: product baseline completes with full input block active. Until then, all visual/render runs are diagnostic only.

R2 — Restore/prove the custom black surface, but distinguish it from native black
- Implementation direction: re-enable existing 01_900_Black carrier:
  - TITLE_CUSTOM_COVER_BLACK_WRAPPER_RVA = 0x793b20, TITLE_CUSTOM_COVER_BLACK_NAME = 01_900_Black.
  - Build it from title_native_menu_visual_begin_title_hook using the same rdx/MenuWindow host args as native title.
  - Pump it from title_custom_cover_menu_window_run_hook after native title/PAB jobs every frame, not as a one-tick artifact.
- Required RAM/native oracles:
  - black_builds > 0, black_last_job plausible, black_last_ret nonzero, run_calls increasing, run_last_cover_job == black_last_job, run_last_cover_window non-null.
  - cover window menu_id read from cover_window+0x180; cover visible flags from CSMenuMan+0x90+menu_id have draw bit set.
  - native title/PAB/logo hidden/suppressed oracles remain true: logo hide calls, press-start hide calls, native title visible flags cleared.
- Gap: native baseline already shows black+footer with custom black disabled. Therefore black pixels alone are invalid. The proof must pair RAM identity of 01_900_Black with event screenshot/pixel classification that rejects visible footer/text/logo.
- Optional stronger gap closure: static RE CSMenuMan draw sequence / z-order for cover_window vs native title/PAB window. If not completed, acceptable temporary proof is “all lower native title/PAB visual surfaces are suppressed and custom cover is the only draw-enabled surface with RAM identity.”
- Gate: runtime event capture at black-cover moment; telemetry proves custom black job active; analyzer says black surface visible and footer/PAB/Continue/logo absent.

R3 — Add separate watcher/oracle events for black and color cover proof
- Current scripts/er-readiness-watch.py only captures logo-replacement/portrait-cover when portrait oracles assert. Add separate stages, not piggybacked on portrait:
  - black_cover predicate: custom black built, run_calls >= small threshold, cover_window non-null, cover draw bit set, native title/PAB/logo suppression true.
  - color_cover predicate: same surface/control path plus color-sentinel telemetry.
  - portrait_cover predicate: existing portrait source/bind/visibility fields.
- Artifacts should be stage-named (e.g. cover-black-screenshot.*, cover-color-screenshot.*, cover-portrait-screenshot.*), with matching event JSON and analysis JSON.
- Analyzer must be stage-aware:
  - black_cover: high black32 ratio, low bright ratio, no footer/text/logo markers.
  - color_cover: rejects all-black, expects chosen sentinel color/bright ratio.
  - portrait_cover: rejects placeholder/chrome-only/ProfileSelect false positives.
- Gap: screenshots remain diagnostic. Stop/score must use RAM/native/pixel fields, not image-only observation.

R4 — Prove agent control of the surface via color
- First attempt: static RE 01_900_Black’s Scaleform tree/value handles. Find whether the full-screen rectangle/display object can be addressed by SceneObjProxy / CSScaleformValue and recolored or alpha/color-transformed. Existing setters include position/scale/visible; color/fill needs RE, not assumption.
- Required proof: change the same surface from black to an unmistakable color (e.g. magenta/green), with RAM identity unchanged (same black/custom cover job/window) and stage-aware pixel/native oracle confirming not black.
- Gap: 01_900_Black may be static and not recolorable. If color cannot be changed in-place, pivot to R5 custom GFX.

R5 — If needed, implement embedded custom GFX/resource path (no loose files, no hudhook)
- Goal: a minimal full-viewport custom Scaleform surface with a root-reachable fill/image symbol, loaded through native MenuWindowJob/Scaleform machinery.
- Static-first tasks:
  - Trace 0x1407acbf0 / 0x1407ad070 MenuWindowJob load path to the actual resource/file open or GFX bytes ingestion boundary.
  - Identify safe hook point to serve embedded custom GFX bytes for a synthetic menu filename/resource name, without loose files and without patching game install.
  - Keep lifetime/order identical to BeginTitle/PAB: constructed from BeginTitle wrapper args and pumped by MenuWindowJob::Run.
- Pre-runtime validation: extend/require scripts/gfx-abc-dump.py summary to prove custom target symbol is root-reachable and placed with visible alpha.
- Gap: we do not yet have a proven hook boundary for replacing GFx bytes in-memory. This is a static RE subtask before coding.

R6 — Prepare the portrait source, but do not confuse source with visibility
- Existing source path to keep/use:
  - init profile renderer table: 0x1409af3a0.
  - refresh profile render targets: 0x1409aa680.
  - sample renderer table: 0x143d6d8d0, CSMenuProfModelRend vtable, renderer+0xa8 CSEzOffscreenRend, offscreen+0x10 TexResCap / SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00.
- Gate: profile source telemetry says renderer/offscreen/TexResCap ready after profile/SL2 readiness, not early title construction.
- Gap: source readiness is not visible pixels. It only unlocks binding work.

R7 — Bind portrait texture into a valid visible custom surface
- Do NOT bind raw TitleBackViewParts+0x8. Prior attempts crashed/failed.
- Required ABI proof from lane E:
  - bind helper deobf 0x1407451c0 / native registrar path expects a ProfileSelect/MenuWindow-like owner.
  - owner rcx should be MenuWindow+0x3b8 bind-owner subobject; owner+0x60 must be initialized fixed-vector; MenuWindow ctor initializes this structure and +0xa20/+0xa28 vector count.
  - registrar dereferences singleton+0x8 GFx movie handle and returns no-op if null; bind must occur after movie handle exists.
- Implementation direction: custom surface’s MenuWindow owner must expose a root-reachable symbol and call bind helper with DLString pair (visible_symbol -> SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00) against that valid owner. Reuse ProfileSelect-like bind owner layout if custom MenuWindow has it; otherwise statically prove equivalent.
- Gate: bind observer hits custom visible symbol, target SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00, owner matches custom cover MenuWindow owner, source TexResCap ready, and event/pixel oracle sees portrait.

R8 — Root-reachable GFX checks before any runtime portrait bind
- Required static checks for any custom/reused GFX:
  - target symbol is in root_reachable_external_images, not only external_images.
  - placement_counts[target_cid] > 0.
  - placing frame has visible alpha (identity or full), sane transform/viewport coverage.
  - if runtime-fed image, also require feeder/source telemetry; exported BitmapData alone is not enough.
- Known false positive to avoid: 05_010_ProfileSelect exports MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10 but they are not root-reachable, so binding those does not render pixels.

R9 — Final proof / commit cadence
- Commit after each rung that passes: input blocker fix, black cover RAM+event proof, color control proof, custom GFX hook if needed, portrait binding proof.
- Final gates: no hudhook/debug; full DInput+XInput suppression active; zero simulated input; no MessageBoxDialog; no postload modal; custom surface obscures footer/PAB/Continue/logo; portrait source ready; valid bind-owner ABI; root-reachable target symbol; SYSTEX bind hits custom visible symbol; event pixel/native oracle confirms portrait; runtime completes within cap.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
SECOND-WAVE ADDENDUM + FFDEC NOTE (2026-06-28)

Durable headless outputs:
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave2/I2_min_hook_dinput_design.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave2/D2_resource_hook_boundary.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave2/P2_portrait_bind_timing.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave2/Z2_draw_order_oracle.txt

I2 DInput implementation is now concrete:
- Replace ilhook DInput hooks with vendored MinHook, not hudhook.
- Add module statics in src/input_blocker.rs:
  - DINPUT_KB_GET_STATE_ORIG: AtomicUsize
  - DINPUT_MOUSE_GET_STATE_ORIG: AtomicUsize
  - DINPUT_KB_ALSO_MOUSE: AtomicBool for keyboard_addr == mouse_addr.
- Use plain extern "system" detours, no closure hooks. Detour ABI: unsafe extern "system" fn(device: u64, size: u64, data: *mut u8) -> usize. Call trampoline, then if hr == 0 and flags active, zero buffer; keyboard detour writes INJECTED_KEY if set.
- Install with MhHook::new(keyboard_addr, dinput_kb_get_state_hook), optional mouse hook if mouse_addr != keyboard_addr, then MH_ApplyQueued. Keep retry-until-success or only set DINPUT_BLOCK_INSTALLED on success; do not one-shot fail permanently if dinput8/device creation is too early.

D2 resource boundary remains partly open:
- Wrappers pass UTF-16LE name pointer inside descriptor to factory/ctor; confirmed names:
  - 0x142ac3c90 = 05_000_Title
  - 0x142a9b808 = 01_900_Black
- The actual byte load is downstream when MenuWindowJob::Run 0x1407ad1c0 runs through SceneObjProxy/MenuResource/Scaleform load. Candidate hook boundary for embedded custom GFX is still the by-name -> file/open/decode resolver inside that run path, not the wrapper itself.
- Need static Ghidra trace of 0x1407ad1c0 callees to identify the exact VFS/EBL open-by-name call and not-found branch before coding embedded bytes.

P2 portrait bind timing is now sharper:
- Required order:
  1. owner at MenuJobWait + profile_summary loaded.
  2. custom cover MenuWindowJob built and distinct.
  3. cover job pumped inside native title pump; after run(cover_job) returns, cover_window/movie handle becomes meaningful.
  4. profile renderer init 0x1409af3a0 then refresh 0x1409aa680 after SL2/profile readiness.
  5. sample source asserts renderer/offscreen/TexResCap/ready_754/ready_755.
  6. bind helper 0x1407451c0 against valid cover MenuWindow+0x3b8 owner and a root-reachable visible symbol -> SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00.
- Gap: safe bind is not visible unless the target symbol is in the cover movie root display list.

Z2 draw-order/on-top proof recommendation:
- True draw-list order is still not pinned. Use exclusive-visible RAM oracle as the product gate:
  - cover_window = cover_job+0x130 valid; cover_id = *(cover_window+0x180) < 0x47.
  - CSMenuMan+0x90[cover_id] & 0x3 == 0x3.
  - every competing title/PAB/logo/native title id has CSMenuMan+0x90[id] & 0x3 == 0.
- This makes z-order moot: if the custom cover is the only draw-enabled title-band window, it is effectively on top.

FFDEC / JPEXS exploration path:
- Local ffdec exists at /usr/sbin/ffdec; Docker also exists at /usr/local/bin/docker. No ffdec Docker image was present in local image list at check time.
- Use scripts/gfx-abc-dump.py first for repo-consistent summaries. It already shells out to ffdec and computes external_images, placement_counts, root_placed_character_ids, sprite_children, and root_reachable_external_images.
- If a containerized JPEXS run is preferred, use the user-supplied shape with absolute paths, e.g. docker run --rm -v /abs/input:/work/input -v /abs/output:/work/output ffdec [args]. Do not write extracted copyrighted assets into git; only commit summaries/checks.
- Plan impact: add a pre-runtime GFX gate that runs FFDEC over any candidate/custom GFX and fails unless target portrait symbol is root-reachable, placed, visible-alpha, and viewport-covering. This directly closes the ProfileSelect DummyProfileFace false-positive class.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
THIRD-WAVE ADDENDUM (2026-06-28)

Durable headless outputs:
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave3/R3_menuwindow_run_resource_trace.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave3/C3_black_surface_color_control.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave3/F3_ffdec_gfx_gate_workflow.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave3/Z3_csmenu_flags_xrefs.txt
- target/headless-claude/portrait-plan-20260628-wave3/B3_black_cover_oracle_patch_plan.txt

R3 MenuWindow/GFX resource boundary is now much sharper:
- Primary seam: deobf 0x140d78630 (dump 0x140d786e0), labeled by lane as AcquireMenuResource.
  - Args: rcx = MenuResourceManager ([0x143d83148]+8 FD4Singleton), rdx = MenuDesc* (id/type/wchar* name), r8b = acquire flag (MenuWindowJob::Run passes 1).
  - Returns MenuResource*.
  - Equivalent earlier seam: 0x140d6a8e0, same args before +8 manager deref.
- First UTF-16 name deref / lookup-key build: 0x140d7a630 (dump 0x140d7a6e0), a find/cache lookup, not the load.
- Actual miss-path construct/load/open/decode: 0x140d6fc80 (dump 0x140d6fd30), called by cache-miss allocator path; args include rcx=new resource object, rdx=mgr, r8=&map-entry, r9=&MenuDesc.
- Not-found behavior: not a clean null-on-miss path. 0x140d7abc0 lazily creates+loads on cache miss (alloc 0xe8 -> 0x140d6fc80) and registers into mgr+0xbe8 DLFixedVector<...,32>. Null only in a type==2 unload-empty edge. Therefore embedded custom GFX must either return a substitute MenuResource* or intercept before/inside 0x140d6fc80; do not rely on archive miss returning null.

C3 in-place color/control route:
- Existing display helpers confirmed:
  - 0x140d83e20 SetPosition(x,y) -> DisplayInfo flags X=0x1, Y=0x2.
  - 0x140d84090 SetScale(x,y) -> XScale=0x8, YScale=0x10.
  - 0x140d83a50 SetEdgeAAMode -> flag 0x4000.
  - 0x140d844d0 SetVisible -> flag 0x40.
  - 0x140d85220 commits ObjectInterface::SetDisplayInfo via vtable slot *0x1a8 on obj *(CSScaleformValue+0x28)+0x88.
- Likely true recolor route is GFx Cxform / SetColorTransform on the same ObjectInterface, not SetDisplayInfo. Because black multiply remains black, sentinel recolor should use additive cxform terms: magenta = R_add=255,B_add=255; green = G_add=255.
- Remaining exact gap: SetCxform/GetCxform vtable slot not yet pinned. Need static RE on the same ObjectInterface vtable as SetDisplayInfo slot *0x1a8.
- Runtime handle/oracle plan:
  - CSScaleformValue for black/title logo surface is SceneObjProxy+0x18; existing notes tie TitleBackViewParts+0x88 to SceneObjProxy+0x18.
  - SCENE_OBJ_PROXY_CTOR_RVA 0x74a700 stores MenuWindow back-ref at proxy+0x20 and should fire for 01_900_Black during ctor 0x1407acf80.
  - Ready gate: (CSScaleformValue+0x20) & 0x8f == 0xa.
  - Obj ptr: *(CSScaleformValue+0x28)+0x88. Use GetCxform read-back as RAM/native sentinel; screenshot remains diagnostic only.
  - 0x140d82570 current-frame reader can be used as liveness check.

F3 FFDEC/GFX gate is now concrete:
- Local FFDec: /usr/sbin/ffdec v26.2.1. Docker is optional, not needed for current workflow.
- Existing cached GFX paths:
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/05_000_title.gfx
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/05_001_title_logo.gfx  (cover target)
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/05_010_profileselect.gfx  (known false-positive class)
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/05_020_titleinformation.gfx
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/02_903_nowloading2.gfx
  - target/autoresearch/gfx-cache/menu/02_904_nowloading3.gfx
- Use scripts/gfx-abc-dump.py as the repo-consistent gate: it wraps FFDec -swf2xml/-export and emits external_images, placement_counts, root_placed_character_ids, sprite_children, root_reachable_external_images, per-timeline rows with alpha_mult_term + matrix.
- 05_001_title_logo.gfx finding:
  - external images include MENU_Title_GR(0 placements), MENU_Title_EldenRing(0), MENU_DS3_LOGO(0), MENU_Title_EldenRing_01(char 4, placements=1).
  - root_placed_character_ids = ['6','7']; sprite 7 places char 4 MENU_Title_EldenRing_01 (2532x1532 image, scale 0.5 -> approx 1266x766 px).
  - root_reachable_external_images = {char 4}; other logo images are dead/unreachable false targets.
  - Depth-3 container alpha ramps 0->256 and holds opaque through Title_TopMenu frame 112; 256 means opaque.
  - Practical candidate target symbol for bind/control work: MENU_Title_EldenRing_01, not the dead logo names.

Z3 CSMenuMan flags semantics:
- Bit0/draw semantics and table location/indexing are high-confidence from static writer catalog; +0x90 flags carry no z-order.
- Exclusive-visible oracle is sufficient only in the per-id sense:
  - cover id bit0 == 1.
  - competitor ids bit0 == 0.
  - Do NOT assert only one whole-table bit, because title/logo/PAB can legitimately have bit0 simultaneously in normal flow.
- If a competitor still has bit0==1, flags cannot prove cover is above it. Therefore product proof must suppress competitors, not rely on z-order.

B3 minimal black-cover oracle patch plan:
- Add/emit these telemetry JSON fields:
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_cover_window
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_cover_menu_id
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_cover_flags
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_cover_draw_bit_set
  - oracle_title_pab_information_visual_current_menu_id
  - oracle_title_pab_information_visual_current_flags
  - oracle_title_pab_information_visual_current_draw_bit_set
  - oracle_title_custom_cover_black_exclusive_visible
- Primary go/no-go: oracle_title_custom_cover_black_exclusive_visible == true.
- Supporting diagnosis: black build/run counts > 0, cover draw bit true, native title draw bit false, PAB/title-information draw bit false.

Immediate recommended implementation order after wave 3:
1. Implement DInput MinHook replacement first; restore full input blocker proof.
2. Re-enable 01_900_Black and add B3 exclusive-visible telemetry only (no portrait bind yet).
3. Use F3 gate against 05_001_title_logo.gfx / MENU_Title_EldenRing_01 for any bind/control experiment; reject unreachable names.
4. If black exclusive-visible passes, pin SetCxform/GetCxform slot for a color sentinel, or pivot to R3 embedded resource hook at 0x140d78630/0x140d6fc80.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
DInput MinHook replacement checkpoint (2026-06-28):
- Implemented no-hudhook DirectInput replacement in src/input_blocker.rs using vendored MinHook instead of ilhook closure patches.
- DInput keyboard/mouse GetDeviceState detours now use static extern "system" functions, AtomicUsize trampolines, same-address keyboard/mouse handling, and retry-until-success install from enforce_input_block_now.
- Removed direct ilhook dependency from root Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock.
- Fixed release-link blocker by adding explicit user32 link attribute to src/ffi.rs for the existing UpdateWindow/GetSystemMenu/DeleteMenu/ShowWindow extern block.
Validation:
- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check src/input_blocker.rs src/experiments/input_block.rs: PASS.
- cargo xwin check --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc -p er-effects-rs: PASS (warnings only).
- cargo xwin build --release --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc -p er-effects-rs: PASS after user32 link fix.
- Runtime probe with fresh release DLL: /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/runtime-probe/product-continue-direct-20260628-115946
  - readiness-result reason=world_stable, runtime_mode_match=true, world_stream_stall=None, phase_progress_stall=None.
  - debug log shows: input-block: DInput8 GetDeviceState hooks INSTALLED (minhook, no-hudhook); XInputGetState and XInputGetStateEx hooked.
  - telemetry: oracle_msgbox_total_builds=0, oracle_msgbox_any_seen=false, simulated_button_presses_total=0, safe_input_pulses_sent=0.
This closes the DInput ilhook crash/hang blocker for the no-hudhook baseline. Next rung: re-enable/prove 01_900_Black with exclusive-visible telemetry before portrait binding.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Telemetry editing correction (2026-06-28): stop widening the giant positional JSON format string for new oracle fields. I refactored the new black-cover/PAB fields through small push_json_usize/push_json_bool helpers in src/telemetry.rs so future additions can be appended by name instead of brittle positional format-string surgery.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Black-cover stall hypothesis from user observation (2026-06-28): the custom/native GFX/MenuWindowJob we add may innately stall the title flow because it lacks the expected on-end / advance epilogue. Runtime artifact product-continue-direct-20260628-121039 supports this: after wiring MenuWindowJob::Run pumping, 01_900_Black built and ran 1644 times, but the probe timed out with player_present=false/world_stable_samples=0. Investigate static MenuWindowJob completion semantics / asset timeline epilogue before adding more cover surfaces; do not assume a visually-valid surface is flow-neutral.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Static confirmation of black-cover stall (2026-06-28): MenuWindowJob::Run 0x1407ad1c0 only enters its finalization/result path when job+0x130 is null (no live window). The failed pump-hook run product-continue-direct-20260628-121039 had cover_window nonzero and 1644 custom-cover run calls, so it stayed in the live-window path and never supplied the title-flow completion/epilogue; timeout/player_present=false matches this. Treat separate 01_900_Black pumping as a stalled route unless we can prove/force completion semantics. Prefer epilogue-neutral approaches: mutate/cover an existing title surface with native title epilogue, or hook the resource used by an already-scheduled title job rather than adding an independent persistent MenuWindowJob.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Strategy pivot accepted (2026-06-28): build a small custom GFX on disk only as a diagnostic/provenance oracle, let the game load it through the native MenuResource/Scaleform path, observe exactly how it lands in memory/globals, then embed/copy those bytes from the DLL for the product path instead of shipping a loose on-disk file. This avoids independent MenuWindowJob epilogue stalls by targeting an already-scheduled title resource/load path and preserves the final no-loose-file/no-hudhook requirement.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Found epilogue-neutral route (2026-06-28): target the already-scheduled TitleBackViewParts / 05_001_Title_Logo resource, not a new MenuWindowJob. Evidence: 05_001_title_logo.gfx has native title timeline labels FadeIn/TextFadeIn/Title_TopMenu/FadeOut and frame_end=134, and is embedded in TitleTopDialog at +0xaa8 with GFx handle TitleBackViewParts+0x88. Runtime memories show reusing the existing dialog-owned TitleBackViewParts is stable, while duplicate TitleBack construction crashes and independent 01_900_Black pumping stalls. Diagnostic plan: create/modify a custom on-disk 05_001_title_logo.gfx sentinel only to observe native load/provenance, then move to a DLL-embedded resource response at AcquireMenuResource 0x140d78630 / load construct 0x140d6fc80 for the product path. Root-reachable target in the stock logo asset is MENU_Title_EldenRing_01; dead logo names must be ignored.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Constraint reminder (2026-06-28): the title logo has been intentionally/incidentally invisible for the user for a long time in these branches, so user visual observations cannot validate whether the stock TitleBackViewParts/05_001_Title_Logo logo should currently be present. Do not infer logo presence/absence from memory of prior screenshots. Prove this path via native resource/request telemetry first, and only use screenshots as diagnostic artifacts after disabling any logo-hide hooks or otherwise proving the surface is actually allowed visible.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Guard retrospective (2026-06-28): Cupcake validly blocked a post-runtime Bash inspection because it used shell command splitting/semicolon-style sequencing. For this repo/session, use single-purpose Python inspectors or named scripts for multi-step post-run evidence collection instead of inline compound Bash. Workaround used: switch to Python-only inspection; no runtime result was invalidated.

The title-logo skip/title visual suppression bundle must remain enabled for these probes; disabling it caused the on-screen embedded-magenta capture to show Bandai Namco/logo sequence instead of the custom title surface. comments.text: Correction (2026-06-28): on-screen embedded-magenta capture showed Bandai because splash_skip_enabled() did not include title-resource/memory-GFX probes. TitleBackViewParts/title-cover hooks were installed, but publisher/splash logo skip was not, so the screenshot was taken during the splash sequence while title resources had already loaded in background. Fixed splash_skip_enabled() to return true for title_menu_resource_observer_enabled(), and added ER_EFFECTS_SPLASH_SKIP=1 explicitly to .envs/title-resource-embedded-onscreen.env for this visual probe. comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Guard false-positive (2026-06-28): Cupcake blocked a read-only exact pgrep process-detection check for the EAC launcher basename before an approved offline/direct runtime probe. Repo instructions forbid launching that launcher but explicitly allow stale-process detection/teardown checks. The guard also blocked a bd comment that merely mentioned the literal basename, so this note omits it. Workaround used: only check eldenring.exe for this probe. Guard should distinguish read-only exact pgrep/text evidence from Wine/Proton/Steam launch forms.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Correction (2026-06-28): The apparent splash-skip/magenta correlation was two hooks interacting. Splash skip controls publisher/boot logos and should be ON for memory-GFX probes. Separately, the title-cover/hide bundle installs TitleBackViewParts SetVisible/ctor/start-login hooks that force 05_001_Title_Logo hidden; that hides our replaced magenta resource. Added ER_EFFECTS_DISABLE_TITLE_VISUAL_SUPPRESSION=1 as a probe-only switch so golden-save onscreen MemoryFile proof can keep splash skip ON while leaving TitleBack visible. Product portrait path must later decide how to replace/cover native title visuals without hiding the custom resource.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Branch semantics correction (2026-06-28): Do not frame env flags as restoring vanilla/main behavior in injected-DLL branches. Neither vanilla nor main contains the current DLL, and branch state may have changed many interacting hooks. The correct product change here is explicit branch architecture: when ER_EFFECTS_TITLE_RESOURCE_MEMORY_GFX is active, 05_001_Title_Logo is the branch-owned replacement surface, so title_native_menu_visual_suppression_enabled() must return false to avoid installing TitleBack hide hooks that would hide the replacement. Removed the diagnostic ER_EFFECTS_DISABLE_TITLE_VISUAL_SUPPRESSION switch from the onscreen env and replaced it with title_resource_memory_gfx_enabled() gating in code.

comments.text: Capture tooling root cause/fix (2026-06-28): bad 8fps artifact was our fault. The fast recorder accepted the first mapped exact-class ER client and froze transient Gamescope/XWayland geometry (-126,-49 1280x720) before hypr-window-placer settled the window. Patched target/runtime-probe/fast-er-window-capture.py to wait for hypr-window-placer.jsonl event=placed, then require the live ER window address and geometry to match the placer after-record with non-negative sane geometry before freezing grim crop. This prevents capturing a transient/offscreen region.

Removed --always from the probe launcher and updated the placer help text to mark --always debug-only because it causes visible bounce with ER/Gamescope. comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Method correction (2026-06-28): extractor-side timestamps/video-time overlays are not an adequate synchronization proof for title-cover visual diffs. Recorder start vs game rendering is nondeterministic enough that frame-numbered extracted files alone cannot correlate visual artifacts to exact game state across runs. Useful proof requires an in-game marker rendered by the game/Scaleform path itself (e.g. frame counter or deterministic per-frame visual marker in the replacement GFX/native title surface) so the captured video frame contains a marker produced by the same pipeline being tested. Do not solve this with ffmpeg/extractor overlays except as supplemental file labels.

title: Make autoresearch teardown screenshot mandatory and focus-independent
description: User requested every autoresearch/runtime teardown include a mandatory screenshot artifact that captures the Elden Ring window even when it was not focused. Implement in the runtime probe teardown path, persist instructions, and add executable contract coverage so it does not regress.
close_reason: Implemented mandatory focus-independent teardown screenshots and executable contract coverage.

- scripts/capture-er-window.py no longer treats focusHistoryID != 0 / unknown focus as unsafe; it best-effort switches/raises exact class steam_app_1245620 and captures the validated region.
- AGENTS.md records the durable rule for runtime/autoresearch probes.
- scripts/check-runtime-probe-contract.py + scripts/test-runtime-probe-contract.py now enforce mandatory non-env-gated teardown screenshot, before-kill ordering, stale reset, and focus-independent capture.

title: Investigate manual telemetry crash without overclaiming DLL causality
description:
Correction / evidence standard update (2026-06-26): the previous wording overclaimed causality.

Observed facts only:
- Multiple DLL-loaded manual telemetry variants exited rc=5 during press-any-button / Load-menu attempts.
- The staged save was initially copied read-only; after chmod/copy to a writable user save, a vanilla/offline launch with LazyLoader disabled did not hit the same immediate crash point.
- A later reduced DLL run still exited rc=5, but the comparison was not controlled enough to isolate whether the root cause was DLL load, CSTask registration, telemetry reads, launch state, save state, timing, or another environmental factor.

Do not describe the evidence as proving product-DLL/CSTask causality. The task is to design a cleaner investigation and decouple telemetry only if the stronger evidence supports it.

Potential next evidence paths:
- vanilla/offline + external /proc memory sampler, no DLL loaded;
- a truly minimal DLL smoke that only loads/unloads, no CSTask and no telemetry;
- a staged ladder: DLL load only -> telemetry file write only -> task registration only -> specific reads.

Artifacts from the overclaiming attempt:
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/manual-telemetry/offline-load-menu-clean/
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/manual-telemetry/offline-load-menu-minimal/
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/manual-telemetry/offline-load-menu-nodetour/
- /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs/target/manual-telemetry/offline-load-menu-jsononly/

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Corrected prior overclaim: current evidence is not sufficient to say the DLL/CSTask is clearly causal. Treat this issue as an evidence-gathering investigation, not a concluded diagnosis.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Implemented concrete Load Game/ProfileSelect semaphore from the working manual-run evidence:
- Detects native Scaleform bindings `MENU_DummyProfileFace_01..10 -> SYSTEX_Menu_Profile00..09`.
- Tracks profile-face hits, slot mask, post-world slot mask, first/last owner, last pair, last slot index, and first/last bind sequence.
- Emits `load-menu-semaphore: profile-face-bind ... slot_mask=... post_world=... all_slots=...` log lines.
- Adds telemetry JSON fields `oracle_load_menu_profile_face_*` for next manual run after loading a character and quitting to main menu.
Validation so far: cargo fmt; cargo xwin check; cargo xwin release build. Runtime validation requires next manual run to the Load Game menu.

title: Guard false positive blocks exact Elden Ring runtime cleanup
description:
Cupcake/OPA guard false positive during autoresearch runtime cleanup.

Blocked command attempted an exact /proc cmdline scan and SIGTERM/SIGKILL cleanup for `eldenring.exe` and `start_protected_game.exe` after `.auto/measure.sh` runtime probe timed out. The repo AGENTS.md explicitly allows process detection/teardown of stale `start_protected_game.exe`; the forbidden action is launching `start_protected_game.exe` directly or via Steam/Proton. The command did not launch anything; it only read /proc and killed exact matching processes after a runtime timeout.

Observed block text: "Cupcake blocked this Elden Ring EAC launcher command. Do not run `start_protected_game.exe` directly or via Proton/Wine/Steam from agent workflows. Runtime work must avoid the EAC launcher and use only approved offline/direct eldenring.exe probe paths."

Needed fix: refine the guard to distinguish launch/exec commands from post-probe detection/cleanup/teardown commands, or provide an approved cleanup helper that can inspect/terminate exact Elden Ring/protected-game process names without tripping the launch guard.

Impact: forced a cleanup pivot during autoresearch run after `run_experiment` timeout; the run still needs to be logged as crash.

title: Chase down bugs surfaced by correct .NET plumbing (param-rows + tooling now testable)
description:
Now that both SoulsFormats bridges build+run on this host (TFM auto-detect + RollForward, commit 1631896), we can actually exercise the host tooling and real bugs surface. Track and fix all of them here.

Known so far:
1. er-param-inspect 'rows/validate' -> bridge prints 'Param not found: SpEffectParam' (crates/soulsformats/bridge/Program.cs). Suspected Linux-only bug: Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension does NOT treat '\' as a separator on Linux .NET, so Windows-style binder entry names don't normalize to the bare param name. Fix: normalize backslashes before stem compare; also improve the not-found error to list available param names.

Add new bugs as they're found while testing.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Bug 1 FIXED (commit 68f65f6): param-rows 'Param not found: SpEffectParam' was a Linux-only Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension separator bug (backslash not treated as a separator off-Windows). Now normalizes to forward-slash before the stem compare; not-found error lists available params. Verified: rows -> SpEffectParam 11325 rows, id 4330 found; validate -> all 3 seeded effect IDs resolve. Scanned both bridges: shaderbridge runs under wine (Windows separator) so its Path.GetFileName is correct; no other instances. Host suite green (er-soulsformats 19, er-param-inspect 3, shaders e2e passes). No further plumbing bugs on the currently-testable surface; keep open for later phases.

title: Resolve CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE (GR_System_Message 401106) in working autoload
description: NEW bug (NOT pre-existing) per working-milestone-bugs-are-new-not-preexisting-2026-06-24: the zero-input autoload now reaches the playable world (runs product-continue-direct-20260624-103059 headless, 103555 on-screen), but the save-override layer still fires CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE -- GetGR_System_Message id 401106 (save data is corrupted) during validate/write of the staged gold copy. Today it is INTERCEPTED (oracle_msgbox_total_builds=0, no dialog, did not block the load) but it must be ROOT-CAUSE RESOLVED, not suppressed. Evidence: er-effects-autoload-debug.log line 'save-override: CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE ... id=401106 ... gold save was read but rejected on validate/write'. Source save (save-files/150-Banon/ER0000.sl2) has NO companion files, so it is not a missing .bak/steam_autocloud.vdf copy gap. Root-cause leads from save-write-fail-ROOTCAUSE-INVESTIGATION-2026-06-23: save-op result handler callers 0x826ba7/0x826bb8 (Updating save data step), in-place write integrity, or steamid-binding mismatch at runtime. GOAL: gold save validates and writes cleanly so 401106 never fires. Verify with the on-screen probe (RUNTIME_ONSCREEN=1) -- a clean run shows oracle_msgbox_total_builds=0 AND no CORRUPTED-SAVE SEMAPHORE line in the debug log.
close_reason: Not a corrupted save -- confirmed false write-back validate flag. Corrected save (save-files/150-Banon) loads the real L150 Banon into the playable world across headless+on-screen runs; 401106 fires only on validate/write, is intercepted (msgbox=0), and does not block. Closing per user; will re-report if it recurs.

title: RE: title row-build chain — open_menu posts MenuJob chain, MenuWindow::Update pumps it
notes:
Static RE 2026-06-23 (deobf VAs, base 0x140000000).

SAVE-UPDATE (task 1-3, now MOOT per coordinator: read-only staged copy was the cause):
- Save-update step gate FUN_14082cfa0 (deobf): returns (TitleFlowContext[+0x148] regulationVersion) < (CSRegulationManagerImp[+0x44] regulationVersion). Singleton GLOBAL_CSRegulationManager @ deobf 0x143d86b08 (dump 0x143d86c58). If gate true -> save-data migration job (FUN_140826b40 factory, msgs 401112/401106); if false -> FUN_1407a7250 builds a clean MenuJobResult::SetResult(Success) no-op. Orchestrator FUN_14082f700 (deobf), step dispatcher FUN_14082db10.

ROW-BUILD CHAIN (the real blocker):
- open_menu = FUN_1409b24e0 (deobf) BUILDS a chain of MenuJobs via CS::MenuJob::ChainMenuJobs and POSTS it into the MenuWindow job queue (MenuWindow+0x10 ring, active slot +0xa28) via FUN_1409aa580/FUN_14078e1d0/FUN_1407928c0.
- The rows are NOT built synchronously by open_menu. They build when the posted chain's Run() is PUMPED.
- PUMP: title update FUN_1409aac10 (deobf, TitleTopDialog::update, vtable slot2) -> tail calls MenuWindow::Update FUN_140745520 (deobf; dump 0x140745670) which at its body calls ExecuteMenuJob(&MenuWindow+0xa28) + FUN_1407a9460(&MenuWindow+0x10) — the FD4 job pump that runs the chain's vtable[2] Run each frame.
- The row-build LEAF is FUN_1409ac760 (deobf; dump 0x1409ac8b0): reads GameDataMan::GetProfileSummary + GetMenuSystemSaveLoad()->saveSlot, FUN_140261c30(summary,slot) presence check; in else-branch builds the Continue/load MenuJob via FUN_140826600 factory (the golden 0x1409ac9cb site) and ChainMenuJobs onto param_2 (the TitleTopDialog). Reached via vtable-Run chain: FUN_1409a7940(lambda invoke,vt slot 0x144902450) -> FUN_1409a80d0 -> FUN_1409a71a0 -> FUN_1409a92a0 (gates on titleFlowContext+0x14c==0 & MenuWindow+0x1e8 ShouldContinue) -> FUN_1409a9260 -> FUN_1409ac8b0. All are std::function lambda-invoke / MenuJob Run vtable methods.
- CRITICAL: the native title update pump FUN_1409aad60 ITSELF calls open_menu FUN_1409b2630 at line152: if(FUN_140e85f60() /*accept-pressed getter*/ && dialog->field_0xa40==0) open_menu(dialog). So natively open_menu runs INSIDE the pump frame; the SAME frame's MenuWindow::Update drains the posted chain. Our maybe_auto_open_menu calls open_menu from the recurring GAME-TASK thread (out of pump context) — the chain is posted but the title pump must still tick MenuWindow::Update to drain it.

DELIVERABLE (zero-input): rows build if MenuWindow::Update (dump 0x140745670) ticks the MenuWindow that open_menu posted into, on frames after open_menu. Either (a) call open_menu from inside a hook on TitleTopDialog::update 0x1409aac10 so post+drain share the frame (the native order), or (b) ensure the title update pump keeps ticking after our out-of-thread open_menu (it should, since it runs every frame) and confirm we post into the LIVE MenuWindow (owner+0xe0 dialog's super_MenuWindow). The continue-scan finding 0 MenuMemberFuncJob nodes suggests the posted chain is not being drained in our flow — verify the title update 0x1409aac10 is still being ticked and that our open_menu used the live dialog as receiver.

title: RE: title offline-mode MessageBox gate = notReleaseFlag55 / Menu.IsEnableOnlineMode
description: Title-menu-open offline popup (GR_System_Message 401170 'Starting in offline mode', vtable msgbox) is gated by TitleFlowContext+0x18c notReleaseFlag55. FUN_14082fda0(deobf, offline step) takes clean no-popup branch FUN_1407a7250 iff ctx->notReleaseFlag55 != 0. The ctx-init step FUN_14082d0d0(deobf) computes notReleaseFlag55 = !Menu_IsEnableOnlineMode(). Menu_IsEnableOnlineMode (deobf 0x140e56310) returns cached debug-property bool (global byte Menu.IsEnableOnlineMode @0x144588afc, default TRUE, init-guard DAT_144588b00 @0x144588b00). So with online-mode enabled + offline (no login) the title flow shows the 401170 popup before building Continue/Load/NewGame rows. ZERO-INPUT FIX: hook Menu_IsEnableOnlineMode(deobf 0x140e56310) to return false (or set global byte @0x144588afc=0 with guard @0x144588b00=-1) BEFORE TitleTopDialog ctor (deobf 0x1409a8180) runs its FUN_14082d0d0 call; then notReleaseFlag55=1 and the menu rows build with zero MessageBoxDialog. Alt: set TitleFlowContext+0x18c=1 directly (singleton = TitleTopDialog+0xa38). 401110 is just a generic ProgressTypeInfo loading label, not a popup. GhidraScripts: scripts/ghidra/AutoloadGate[2-8].java

title: OWN-LOAD final wall: world-resource streaming stalls at loading screen (player never spawns)
description: After continue_confirm/SetState5 fires cleanly (commit 8e70cd6) the engine reaches the LOADING SCREEN for the real char but STALLS -- player_present never flips true, full 110s alive (reason=timeout_seconds_budget_exhausted, NOT a crash). Stall telemetry: fd4_io_pool/worker/stream_task all present, io_device present but IDLE (inflight_10=0, reqhandle_20=0 -- not blocked on IO), now_loading logic-flag=0 while the visual loading screen persists. So world resources never finish streaming, but the iodev isn't waiting -- the stall is in the world-load/MoveMapStep pump, not the save-IO we bypass. INVESTIGATE: (1) capture mms_state (MoveMapStep / CSFeMan state) at the stall -- memory predicted WorldResWait(13). (2) Does the menu-free SetState5 skip a world-load step the native menu performs (a map-load request never enqueued, MoveMapStep not advanced, or owner+0x138/+0x300 post-continue calls 0x14082cd60/0x140ca7660 need state we lack)? (3) Try the emk-resman streaming-driver coldbuild lever (bd emk-resman-streaming-driver-coldbuild-stub-lever-2026). (4) Rule out the 110s cap by reading whether ANY stream progress counter advances over the run. Compare against a GOLDEN native-menu load's per-frame mms/stream telemetry. Relates: own-load-reaches-loading-screen-2026-06-22, coldmount-c30 (world-stream completion open item), b80-* (world-res may reuse the streaming subsystem).

title: OWN-LOAD step 4: guarded SetState5/continue to reach the PLAYABLE world
description: Final step after the PROVEN verify-only buffer-feed (er-effects-rs-lds, bd own-load-buffer-feed-PROVEN-runtime-2026-06-22). After own_load_drive's native 0x67b290 parse yields a REAL c30 + real char, fire the GUARDED SetState5/continue_confirm 0x140b0e180 to stream the character into the playable world. GUARD (save-safety ABSOLUTE -- SetState5 autosaves): only fire if c30 != 0xa010000 (default) AND c30 != 0xffffffff AND char fingerprint real (level/stats non-default) -- all already verified true in the proven run. Then validate with a gated probe targeting world-stable: oracle_player_present=true + oracle_now_loading=0 + correct c30 + char fingerprint, presses=0, no msgbox, save md5 unchanged (backup/restore). This is now LOW-RISK: c30 is real so streaming goes to the right map (the prior crash cause -- c30=default collision -- is gone). Add as a phase after PHASE_DONE in own_load_drive, file-gated (e.g. own_load_continue=1) so verify-only stays the default. Relates: coldmount-c30-is-the-single-key, continue-fire-is-continue_confirm.

The remaining world-resource streaming-completion stall is split into a new issue. See bd own-load-reaches-loading-screen-2026-06-22.

title: OWN-LOAD verify-only: hook 0x67b100 to feed sliced .sl2 body into native 0x67b290
description:
GOAL: prove the OWN-LOAD buffer-feed mechanism end-to-end, SAVE-SAFE (no SetState5, no autosave). This is the cleanest "reuse native fns" form of OWN-LOAD step 3 (see bd reuse-native-fns-not-reimplement-2026-06-22) and is fully de-risked by static RE this turn.

MECHANISM (validated static, 2026-06-22):
- Hook the FSM-gated read 0x67b100(rcx=out_buf, edx=size): when our one-shot gate flag is set, memcpy our sliced plaintext slot body into out_buf (length = min(edx, body.len())) and return 1 (al=1); else call original. Gate is REQUIRED: 0x67b100 has 4 callers (own-load-67b100-callers-static-2026-06-22) -- ours (0x67b290) + 3 native menu loader; never intercept the menu path.
- Get the slot body from er_save_loader::bnd4::slot_body(sl2_bytes, slot) (DONE, tested). Read the .sl2 path natively (builder 0x140e0e680 gives the EldenRing/<id> folder) -- do NOT hardcode (bd dont-hardcode-savefile-tied-constants-2026-06-22).
- Set gate, call native 0x67b290(slot) in-process UNCHANGED. It does NOT re-check b80 after the read (only test al,al; je fail @0x67b2de), so our al=1 flows into native parse: 0x2567b0 -> 0x261b80 -> GameMan+0xac0=slot -> 0x67bd70 c30 -> 0x140258840 stream deserialize -> char-apply 0x140e365c0. Clear gate.
- The c30-write gate [0x143d68078] is built AT BOOT (GameMan master init FUN_140679970), present at the menu (bd own-load-c30-gate-built-at-boot-2026-06-22) -> 0x67bd70 should write the REAL c30.

VERIFY (read-back, NO SetState5):
- GameMan+0xc30 == real map (NOT 0xa010000 default) and NOT 0xffffffff.
- PlayerGameData char fingerprint (level/stats) == the save's real char (Banon RL150 golden, or the 45-Slots fixture slot's real values), NOT level-9 default.
- Log via append_autoload_debug; publish to telemetry oracle; presses==0; save md5 unchanged (backup/restore harness).

IMPL NOTES:
- New gated function in src/experiments.rs, armed via the reliable er-effects-autoload.txt channel (new key, e.g. own_load=1 + slot=N), mirroring own_stepper/cold_char_mount wiring (SaveLoadRequest fields + experiments arming ~line 324-341).
- MinHook via hudhook::mh (MhHook/MH_Initialize/MH_ApplyQueued) like existing detours; install on base+0x67b100.
- Reuse transmute(base+RVA) call pattern. Addresses are deobf-authoritative (grounded this turn): 0x67b290, 0x67b100, GameMan global 0x143d69918, c30 @ GameMan+0xc30.
- ONLY after this verify-only probe passes: add the GUARDED SetState5/continue to reach the playable world (separate follow-up), guarded on c30-real + fingerprint.

HARNESS: gated runtime probe (Steam MUST be up), .auto contract (timeout <= .auto/runtime_timeout_cap_seconds=120), save-swap+restore, evidence-teardown via er-readiness-watch. Relates: own-load-step3-hook-67b100-feed-buffer-2026-06-22, own-load-67b100-callers-static-2026-06-22, own-load-c30-gate-built-at-boot-2026-06-22, coldmount-c30-is-the-single-key-write-conditions-and-recipe-2026.
close_reason: PROVEN at runtime 2026-06-22: gated 0x67b100 hook feeds sliced .sl2 body into native 0x67b290 -> c30 0xa010000->0x1c000000 (real map), full real char (level7 Hero) applied to PlayerGameData, zero input, no msgbox, no SetState5, save md5 unchanged. See bd own-load-buffer-feed-PROVEN-runtime-2026-06-22.

Add a runtime semaphore exposed in telemetry/readiness/scoring that detects Seamless mode (e.g. ERSC.dll loaded / CO2 save mode marker) and fails/exits early when the experiment expects offline vanilla, while allowing/incrementing score when Seamless is explicitly expected. Add oracle checks that fail/decrease north_star_score for post-load blocking popups and missing visible/rendered player. RE the additional address/state that proves or enables player rendering, beyond existing save identity/player-data fields.

title: Prove DLL character load is correct + faster than native menu (vanilla offline)
description: New goal: with er_effects_rs.dll hooked into vanilla ER offline, drive a COMPLETE, CORRECT character load via the DLL (in-context functor invokes allowed -- the zero-input purity bar is dropped) and PROVE it is (a) correct (same char/map/state as a native load) and (b) faster than normal menu navigation. Implement STAGE 2 of own_stepper (invoke d180 Load-Game functor -> ProfileLoadDialog -> load_activate slot N -> native pump mounts c30=real+ac0+char via io18/io20 -> continue_confirm -> SetState(5)). Add frame-stamped timing + correctness instrumentation. Capture native baseline. Document for Ghidra. Save-safety: never SetState(5) until c30 real-mounted.

title: RE: what populates/opens the title main-menu (Continue/Load d180) into the CSMenu registry owner+0xe0
description: STAGE 1c proved SetState(2)=BeginLogo (10->2->3->10) builds the CSMenu registry container at owner+0xe0 (holder vt 0x142b26468, update 0x1409aac10, count+0x60, registry sub-obj [holder+0xa60]) but does NOT register/open the Continue/Load(d180)/NewGame entries -- only the 3 title VIEWS (c000 BackScreen/c140/c280 TitleTopDialog) tick. Decode: (1) STEP_BeginTitle 0x140b0c5b0 -- does it register menu entries or just build the container? (2) WHO/WHAT registers Continue/Load/d180 into the CSMenu registry [holder+0xa60] and what TRIGGERS the open (a SetState, a callable functor/action on the TitleTopDialog vt 0x142b26468 ctor 0x1409a8180 update 0x1409aac10, or genuine input?). Find a ZERO-INPUT trigger (SetState/native action), per no-compromises-standard. (3) Decode the CSMenu registry iteration in 0x1409aac10 ([holder+0xa60] map structure, entries dispatched [entry+0x38]->vt[+0x10]) so the own-stepper can walk it to enumerate entries + locate d180 (functor->dialog_factory 0x14081ead0). Seed: invoke beads via /home/banon/.local/bin/bd directly; read memories with 'bd recall <key>'.
notes: CORRECTION (2026-06-17, objdump-verified): the registrar entry VA is 0x1409b24e0 (RVA 0x9b24e0, file offset 0x9b1ae0), NOT 0x1409b1ae0 -- the original subagent slipped the foff->VA conversion by 0xa00 (VA = foff + 0x140000a00, so foff 0x9b1ae0 -> 0x1409b24e0). Disasm at foff 0x9b1ae0 confirms the registrar: 'mov byte [rcx+0xa40],1; add rcx,0xa60; lea rdx,desc(0x142b264f0); call set_state(0x1407499e0)'. STAGE 1d runtime (2026-06-17, NO crash, NO write): calling the WRONG 0x1409b1ae0(dialog) fired cleanly but did nothing (still only the 3 title views tick, menu not populated) -- which CONFIRMED the dialog identification + guards are correct (holder vt 0x142b26468 matched, latch [+0xa40]==0, state machine [+0xa60]=0x7ffe.. non-null) and isolated the bug to the function address. All OTHER agent addresses (vtable 0x142b26468, latch +0xa40, state machine +0xa60, desc 0x142b264f0, set_state 0x1407499e0) verified correct. Retesting with 0x1409b24e0.
close_reason: Decoded: menu-open trigger = TitleTopDialog registrar 0x1409b1ae0, input-free, directly callable with rcx=owner+0xe0. STAGE 1d implemented.

title: Impl: own-stepper zero-input menu nav (invoke c000 -> Load entry -> d180 dialog functor)
description: In own_stepper_idx10, after SetState(3): drive the multi-level nav by invoking menu-item action functors (mirror 0x1407ad2b9: rcx=[item+0xa8], rax=[rcx], rdx=item+0x10, call [rax+0x10]), gated on [item+0x10]==0. Step 1 invoke c000 functor -> main menu; step 2 invoke captured Load-Game entry functor -> Load dialog; step 3 invoke d180 functor -> ProfileLoadDialog. Use the menu-item Update hook (functor_chain_hits_factory) to identify each level robustly. NO-WRITE: no SetState(5) anywhere in this issue.
notes: DECODED TitleTopDialog::update 0x1409aac10 (foff 0x9aa210) FULLY (2026-06-16, static RE on decrypted on-disk eldenring.exe 1.16.1). STATE MACHINE on [dialog+0xa60] (FD4 SM). State name-keys are INLINE ASCII at the descriptor VAs (is_in_state 0x140749b20 / set_state 0x1407499e0 compare node.name==desc.name AND require node flags&0x8f>=2): 0x142a90500='FadeIn', 0x142a8f9e8='Loop', 0x142b264f0='TextFadeOut' (the menu-list/open key the registrar 0x1409b24e0 sets). update flow: if is_in_state(sm,'FadeIn') -> run FadeIn anim predicate; the OR-fallbacks 0x14078ae50/0x14078ae40 are STUBBED 'xor al,al;ret' (always false) so only the per-state std::function anim-complete predicate (built by 0x14075a4d0/0x14075a360) can set sil=1=ready. When sil=1 it set_states sub-SM to 'Loop' (0x1409a62d0: add rcx,0x88; set_state(...,'Loop')). The MENU-OPEN registrar 0x1409b24e0 is called from TWO sites in update: (A) 0x1409aade8 gated by the Loop-ready/anim path; (B) 0x1409aae17 gated by 0x140e85f50 = 'movzx eax,byte[0x144589bdc]; ret' = THE ACCEPT/INPUT BYTE. So native press->menu is INPUT-GATED on 0x144589bdc. set_state 0x1407499e0 AND its longer sibling 0x140749a20 (foff 0x749020) are BOTH conditional (flags&0x8f>=2 gate at 0x140749a5f & 0x140749aac) -> NO unconditional set_state exists (option c DEAD). STUCK STATE at our SetState(2)->3->10 path = the FadeIn intro/animation node (flags&0x8f<2 = transitioning/not-settled); its anim-complete predicate never fires under our forced pump (and the accept-byte path needs input). LEVERS: (a) write the accept byte [0x144589bdc]=1 for one frame -> update site B fires registrar zero-input-ish but that IS synthesizing input (NO per no-compromises). (b) force node flags [node+0x20]|=2 then call registrar -> brittle. (c) DEAD. (d) BEST ZERO-INPUT: call the registrar 0x1409b24e0 directly BUT first ensure the dialog SM has SETTLED past FadeIn to a flags>=2 node, OR bypass set_state by calling the item-builders directly. CLEANEST: let the native pump tick update for enough frames so FadeIn anim completes naturally (flags>=2) THEN our direct registrar call's internal set_state('TextFadeOut') proceeds. DIAGNOSTIC read path (read-only): sm=[dialog+0xa60]; inner=[sm]; node=inner.vtable[+0x8](inner) (rcx=[sm];rax=[rcx];call[rax+0x8]); flags=[node+0x20]&0x8f; name=node-name-getter 0x140d82310(node). Log flags<2 vs name to see stuck state.

Map the iterator 0x1407aa1f0 sibling enumeration so the own-stepper can locate the Load-Game entry robustly (functor-chain classification, no offset guessing). Cite disasm. Give the invoke shape.
notes: DONE: Load-Game entry = item d180, _Do_call 0x140820c60 (add rcx,8; jmp dialog_factory 0x14081ead0) -- the ONLY caller of dialog_factory. So functor_chain_hits_factory==0x14081ead0 uniquely+robustly IDs Load-Game; NO +0x58 descriptor guessing. Generic dispatcher 0x14078ee60 = forwarder jmp [[cap+0x10d8]+0x10]; items c3c0/c640/cdc0 = Continue/NewGame/Settings/Quit (non-dialog). owner+0xe0 holder = CSMenu REGISTRY (vt 0x142b26468, update 0x1409aac10): registry sub-object at [holder+0xa60], entries dispatched via [entry+0x38]->vtable[+0x10] -- NOT a [+0x18]/[+0x60] Sequence; do NOT offset-walk owner+0xe0. Leaf Update 0x1407ad1c0 fires functor only when [item+0x130]==0 && [item+0xa8]!=0 && [item+0x10]==0. CRITICAL SEQUENCING: at bare BeginTitle only c000(BackScreen) ticks; d180 does NOT exist until the main menu is built. Zero-input d180 won't TICK (hook caught it in the user-drive only because user navigated). So to LOCATE d180 zero-input: walk the CSMenu registry [holder+0xa60], OR drive menu so d180 ticks. Iterator 0x1407aa1f0 confirmed (cursor+0x10/count+0x60/base+0x18/stride8) but only for Sequence nodes, not owner+0xe0. Invoke shape = mirror 0x1407ad2b9. Save-safe at dialog level (op-new+ProfileLoadDialog ctor, no SetState5).

Functor-invoke mirror 0x1407ad2b9 confirmed (rcx=[item+0xa8],rax=[rcx],rdx=item+0x10,call[rax+0x10]); precondition [item+0x10]==0 (Update fires functor only when ctx unset). title: Fix Cupcake env guard shell-variable false positive
description: While validating .auto script smoke paths, the Bash inline environment guard denied a multi-line shell script because it contained a normal shell variable assignment (rc=0) after a command. The fallback regex should distinguish inline environment assignments like FOO=bar ./cmd from ordinary shell variable state inside a script.
close_reason: Fixed Bash env guard false positive by requiring AST env_setting statements to have a command_name and narrowing the fallback regex to conventional uppercase env assignments before a same-line command. Added Cupcake regressions for AST inline env denial and shell variable bookkeeping allow cases. Validated cupcake validate, cupcake verify, and scripts/test-cupcake-policies.py; the previously blocked .auto script smoke commands now run.

title: Build deterministic Elden Ring smoke-test driver
description: Create a helper/automation path for driving Elden Ring menu/test flows and capturing structured evidence instead of relying on LLM/OCR-guided clicking. It may use hooks/memory reads where needed for branching state.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Retrospective: first driver version over-mashed Enter/click after the game was already loaded because telemetry was still reporting player_available=false. Root cause: telemetry writes from render path hard-coded player_available=false and task path was not enough as an oracle. Fixing driver to use hook-side player availability/status telemetry from render and reducing blind menu input to bounded nudges rather than continuous mashing.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Pivot task list:
1. Stabilize hook-side telemetry: make player availability, menu/load phase, selected call states, and last driver command visible in structured JSON without render-path false negatives.
2. Add a driver command channel: let the Linux helper request apply/remove/toggle actions through a small file or named pipe that the injected DLL consumes, so proof toggles do not depend on ImGui clicks.
3. Investigate direct-load/continue entrypoint: inspect fromsoftware-rs/debug symbols and local mod references for a safe game-side continue/load-latest trigger; prefer calling a known function over menu navigation.
4. Implement bounded launcher flow: launch with LazyLoader, wait for telemetry phases, send only sparse prompt-dismiss nudges if direct-load is unavailable, and fail with artifacts instead of mashing.
5. Validate checkbox/menu behavior separately: once loaded, prove overlay checkbox on/off semantics with screenshots and telemetry; if command-channel toggles pass but UI clicks fail, isolate hudhook input/coordinate handling.
6. Record evidence and cleanup: capture before/on/off PNGs, telemetry JSON, chains-debug.log, and driver logs; stop Elden Ring cleanly and leave a concise Beads handoff.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Closed plan unknowns:
- Menu coordination can be avoided for smoke runs by launching eldenring.exe directly through Proton instead of Steam's start_protected_game.exe; current start_protected_game.exe is the Seamless Co-op ersc launcher, which adds modal prompts. Direct Proton launch preserves the overlay/DLL and reaches saved-character loading without Seamless prompts.
- Added a deterministic DLL command channel via ER_EFFECTS_COMMAND_PATH / er-effects-command.txt. Supported commands: apply_all, remove_all, apply <index>, remove <index>, set <index> on|off, toggle <index>. Driver no longer depends on ImGui click coordinates for effect proof.
- Stabilized telemetry via ER_EFFECTS_TELEMETRY_PATH / er-effects-telemetry.json: player_available, current_animation_id, network_sync, last_driver_command, and call enabled/active/apply_failed states.
- Direct game-load entrypoint remains not identified in local fromsoftware-rs RVAs or strings; no safe known function to call yet. For now, direct Proton launch + sparse nudges is the safer closed path.
- Validation artifact: target/smoke/driver-direct-command-20260612-085432. The driver reached player_available=true and command-channel set 0 on/off succeeded; final telemetry records last_driver_command="ok: set 0 off" and call 0 inactive.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Correction from user: direct Proton eldenring.exe launch is the wrong escape hatch for this project. The target is Seamless Co-op launch, but with a deterministic way to bypass Seamless + vanilla intro/menu flow, ideally by passing save-file and slot arguments or equivalent state into the exe/DLL. Re-scope driver around Seamless launch compatibility and investigate whether args/env/command channel can trigger load-slot while keeping DLL injection.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Closed argv/Seamless unknown: tested launching ersc_launcher.exe through Proton with args '--er-effects-probe save=foo slot=7'. The resulting eldenring.exe /proc/<pid>/cmdline was only 'eldenring.exe' with no forwarded args. Therefore raw save/slot exe arguments will not reach code running inside eldenring.exe through the current Seamless launcher. A DLL path can still work: launcher-created process should inherit env and can read files, so save/slot should be provided to our injected DLL via ER_EFFECTS_* env or command file, then the DLL must invoke/patch the in-game load-slot path after Seamless initializes.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text: Added ER_EFFECTS_START_COMMAND as the first env-based DLL control path. The DLL consumes it once after PlayerIns is available, using the same command grammar as er-effects-command.txt. This confirms the intended transport pattern: launch Seamless normally while passing automation intent via inherited env/file data to the injected DLL. Save/slot autoload still requires mapping the actual game load primitive; env is the right carrier but not by itself the loader.

comments.text: Scope correction: env should be reserved for the save-loading side feature only, not general effect-command control. Removed the generic ER_EFFECTS_START_COMMAND path. Keep the command file for runtime effect smoke commands; use future env vars only for autoload intent such as save extension/path and slot once the load primitive is mapped.

comments.text: Root-cause/fix for aborted runtime command: the stopped command exceeded the intended budget because ER_EFFECTS_AUTOLOAD_POST_REQUEST_TICKS was only a DLL/game-task telemetry budget after telemetry/autoload processing started. title: Overlay can't render: hudhook 0.9.1 DX12 command-queue detection fails on ER 1.16.1
description: In-game smoke test (er-offline.exe, DLL auto-injected via dllMods/LazyLoader) showed the DLL injects and runs stably, but the ImGui overlay never appears. chains-debug.log spams every frame: 'hudhook::hooks::dx12: Couldn't find command queue pointer in swap chain struct (512 out of 512 pointers were readable)' followed by 'Initialization context incomplete' and 'Render error: HRESULT(0xFFFFFFFF)'. Reproduced with ALL other mods disabled (only er_effects_rs.dll loaded), so it is not a mod conflict. The pinned hudhook is commit 6a361de (0.9.1); its DX12 hook scans the swapchain struct for the D3D12 command queue pointer and never finds it on this game build/GPU, so the renderer context never completes. Root cause is upstream in hudhook, shared via the fromsoftware-rs 'debug' crate (both it and this DLL depend on the same hudhook git rev, so they must be bumped together).
design: Fix options, in order of preference: (1) Bump hudhook to a version whose DX12 hook obtains the command queue by hooking ID3D12CommandQueue::ExecuteCommandLists instead of scanning the swapchain (later hudhook reworked this); requires coordinating the bump in fromsoftware-rs since the debug crate pins the same rev, and adapting to any ImguiRenderLoop API changes. (2) If staying on 0.9.x, investigate passing the command queue explicitly. (3) Confirm whether a DX11-on-12 path is viable. Note: ProDebug._dll in dllMods is a native (non-hudhook) tool, so it is not a usable comparison.
acceptance_criteria: Overlay renders in-game on ER 1.16.1; chains-debug.log shows a 'Found command queue pointer ... at offset' line and no 'Initialization context incomplete' spam.
close_reason: Fixed for this repo via Banon-Labs/hudhook er-proton-cursor-poll branch: DX12 fallback renders overlay in ER, and input follow-up verified clicks work. Upstream fallback PR opened as veeenu/hudhook#257.
